Title: AI Scientist - AI Governance
Place: Ballerup Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Enterprise IT and Quality

Ballerup, Denmark | London, UK

Shape the future of AI in healthcare. Build cutting-edge AI platforms that process billions of tokens monthly and empower thousands of innovators across Novo Nordisk to bring AI-driven solutions to patients worldwide. The role can also be based in London, UK.

Your new role

As an AI Scientist in AI Governance and Enablement in Enterprise AI, you'll play a pivotal role in ensuring that AI is developed and deployed responsibly at Novo Nordisk. Working at the intersection of responsible AI, risk management, and enterprise governance, you'll apply scientific rigour to evaluate, monitor, and improve AI systems throughout Novo Nordisk value chain that impact patients and innovators across the organisation.

Your Responsibilities Will Include


Translating responsible AI principles, regulatory requirements, and governance policies into practical methods and tools suitable for enterprise-wide usage
Continuously adapting practices towards new forms of AI systems (also the next wave of Agentic AI) for safe, reliable, and compliant development and usage
Enabling "AI to govern AI" capabilities, leveraging LLMs and agentic tools to support scalable, responsible AI development and deployment
Designing and applying scientific frameworks to assess AI model risk, bias, fairness, and performance across production AI systems
Collaborating with AI engineers, Legal experts, IT risk, cybersecurity, research & product teams, and business stakeholders to embed governance directly into AI platforms and workflows
Supporting citizen developers and a broad range of users in adopting AI safely and responsibly


Your new department

In Enterprise IT & Quality, you will be part of a business area that drives impact across the full Novo Nordisk value chain. We strive to stay close to our stakeholders in the business to ensure we deliver on their needs, helping reach millions of people living with serious chronic diseases. Our focus is on being at the forefront within our three areas; ensuring the highest quality for patients, being a trusted tech leader and an environmentally responsible business.

Within Enterprise IT sits Enterprise AI, a dynamic organization of AI Engineers, AI Scientists, and Platform experts dedicated to delivering impactful AI tooling and cases across Novo Nordisk. Our area comprises four key teams: Forward Deployed AI Engineering (reimagining core Novo Nordisk processes with AI), AI Core (providing custom AI platforms processing billions of tokens monthly), Citizen AI and BI Platforms (delivering SaaS platforms that fuel AI and analytics), and AI Governance (ensuring Responsible AI in our development practices). We're cloud-native, primarily working with Databricks, AWS, and MS Azure, and we embrace agility, diverse thinking, and continuous learning in a culture built on feedback, honesty, and fun.

Your Skills & Qualifications

We're looking for an AI Scientist who combines solid scientific expertise with the ability to navigate complex governance and regulatory landscapes. You'll bring with you:


Strong background in AI/ML science, characterised by a Master’s degree (or equivalent experience). with proven experience bringing 3+ generative AI, LLM, or agent-based projects in larger organizations from pilot into production
Experience with AI Governance concepts, including model evaluation, risk assessment and risk classification, bias detection, fairness metrics, explainability, and a pragmatic approach to risk-benefit balance
Demonstrated ability to lead projects and manage multidisciplinary stakeholders, translating complex technical and governance topics for both technical and non-technical audiences
Strong knowledge of governance principles, AI regulatory frameworks, and relevant regulations (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, EU AI Act, ISO 42001)
Ability to navigate complex regulatory environments and translate them into pragmatic, scalable solutions to overcome governance challenges
Enabling a pragmatic mindset, with the ability to work effectively in complex, cross-functional environments and influence across organisational boundaries without formal authority
Continuous curiosity for the evolving AI landscape, with a demonstrated ability to translate external AI innovation into pragmatic internal governance rules and capabilities


Additionally, It Would Be An Advantage If You Have


Experience working in regulated or highly governed industries (e.g., pharma, finance, healthcare)
Proficiency with AI-driven development workflows and CI/CD pipelines, including Docker-based environments and cloud platforms (MS Azure, AWS)
Experience with AI agents and multi-agent systems, including hands-on experience with agentic frameworks (Microsoft Agent Framework, Google ADK, or similar)
A pragmatic approach to risk-benefit balance
Experience collaborating with security, risk, or compliance stakeholders


Working at Novo Nordisk

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

What We Offer

At Novo Nordisk, you’ll join a global healthcare company with a unique culture and strong results. We offer continuous learning, career development, and benefits tailored to your life and career stage.

Salary: For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 651,000.00 to 956,900.00 DKK, corresponding to the level of the position.

The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate’s skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.

Incentives and Benefits: The salary package may include short-term and/or long-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.

Learn more about our Reward Philosophy here.

More information

If you want to know more, please reach out to Margi Sheth (rgsw@novonordisk.com), Head of AI Governance & Enablement.

Deadline

31 May 2026. Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis.

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

Title: Machine Learning Researcher
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Imagine working with cutting-edge machine learning models, crunching high frequency data sets and sparring with exceptional and fun colleagues, all while taking part in scaling our business even further.




Does this sound highly appealing to you? Then you might be our new Machine Learning Researcher at Alipes.




At Alipes, we have been the market leader in automating the financial markets since we started back in 2008, shaping a fully automatic natural language processing system for reading and interpreting financial news.




To accomplish our mission of being the best at what we do, we understand the importance of elevating our predictive modeling standards to the highest levels. Your work will involve leading the efforts in creating a world class modeling workflow, enabling the team to seamlessly turn massive data sets of financial information into mathematical models that accurately predict movements in the financial markets.




In doing so, you will get a chance to tackle a variety of software infrastructure challenges, such as:

Development and optimization of calibration and benchmarking frameworks
Building out and implementing best practices for data preparation and dataset generation pipelines
Prototyping and releasing novel predictive architectures, especially with respect to time-series models.




You will get instant validation of your work and experience short feedback cycles, where going from inception to deployment can be a matter of hours. There will be no red tape to cut and no sales people to consult.




About the team

All of this will happen in an informal atmosphere where technical discussions are valued, and you are encouraged to take ownership of, and pride in, your work. You will impact the direction of the team, prioritize your work and choose the tools that get the job done. We are a tight-knit modeling team with fourteen passionate quantitative researchers covering 6 nationalities – eleven PhD’s and three MSc’s with expertise in statistical analysis, mathematical modeling and machine learning. We enjoy collaboration and are always willing to lend a helping hand. We are working alongside two other teams of software developers and traders that implement and conceive the mathematical models together with us. What makes this constellation work great is that all of the individual team members write code and understand that a seemingly trivial change can actually require a lot of work, and vice versa.




About you

Fluency within mathematics and statistics.
Experience working with the full Machine Learning stack from data generation through model calibration and real-life validation and monitoring.
Programming experience with Python, in particular data-wrangling and numerical programming.
Proficiency with computer science fundamentals.
A scientific and inquisitive mind.




Nice to have

Experience working with ‘out-of-core’ datasets.
Experience working with tools like PyTorch, Tensorflow, XGBoost and/or Catboost.
Programming experience with languages like C# and C/C++.
PhD or MSc degree in engineering, physics, computer science, mathematics or economics.




What we can offer you

Competitive benefits, including pension, health insurance, and 30 days of vacation.
A modern, harbour front office in Copenhagen (Nordhavn) with a flexible hybrid work model.
A flat hierarchy with high levels of trust, autonomy, and freedom to make decisions.
A valued role with unique responsibilities and plenty of room for creativity.
Fast feedback and recognition of your contributions in a high-growth company.
Strong team spirit - successes are celebrated with company events and trips.




Practicalities

To apply, please send us your CV and, in lieu of providing a cover letter, concisely answer the questions on the application. If your profile looks like a good match, we will get in touch to coordinate an online technical test as a first step in the interview process.




Should you have questions about the role that you would like clarified before applying, you're welcome to send an email to quant_jobs@alipescapital.com. Please note that applications should not be sent to the email address but rather via the job link.

Imagine working with cutting-edge machine learning models, crunching high frequency data sets and sparring with exceptional and fun colleagues, all while taking part in scaling our business even further.


Does this sound highly appealing to you? Then you might be our new Machine Learning Researcher at Alipes.


At Alipes, we have been the market leader in automating the financial markets since we started back in 2008, shaping a fully automatic natural language processing system for reading and interpreting financial news.


To accomplish our mission of being the best at what we do, we understand the importance of elevating our predictive modeling standards to the highest levels. Your work will involve leading the efforts in creating a world class modeling workflow, enabling the team to seamlessly turn massive data sets of financial information into mathematical models that accurately predict movements in the financial markets.


In doing so, you will get a chance to tackle a variety of software infrastructure challenges, such as:

  • Development and optimization of calibration and benchmarking frameworks
  • Building out and implementing best practices for data preparation and dataset generation pipelines
  • Prototyping and releasing novel predictive architectures, especially with respect to time-series models.


You will get instant validation of your work and experience short feedback cycles, where going from inception to deployment can be a matter of hours. There will be no red tape to cut and no sales people to consult.


About the team

All of this will happen in an informal atmosphere where technical discussions are valued, and you are encouraged to take ownership of, and pride in, your work. You will impact the direction of the team, prioritize your work and choose the tools that get the job done. We are a tight-knit modeling team with fourteen passionate quantitative researchers covering 6 nationalities – eleven PhD’s and three MSc’s with expertise in statistical analysis, mathematical modeling and machine learning. We enjoy collaboration and are always willing to lend a helping hand. We are working alongside two other teams of software developers and traders that implement and conceive the mathematical models together with us. What makes this constellation work great is that all of the individual team members write code and understand that a seemingly trivial change can actually require a lot of work, and vice versa.


About you

  • Fluency within mathematics and statistics.
  • Experience working with the full Machine Learning stack from data generation through model calibration and real-life validation and monitoring.
  • Programming experience with Python, in particular data-wrangling and numerical programming.
  • Proficiency with computer science fundamentals.
  • A scientific and inquisitive mind.


Nice to have

  • Experience working with ‘out-of-core’ datasets.
  • Experience working with tools like PyTorch, Tensorflow, XGBoost and/or Catboost.
  • Programming experience with languages like C# and C/C++.
  • PhD or MSc degree in engineering, physics, computer science, mathematics or economics.


What we can offer you

  • Competitive benefits, including pension, health insurance, and 30 days of vacation.
  • A modern, harbour front office in Copenhagen (Nordhavn) with a flexible hybrid work model.
  • A flat hierarchy with high levels of trust, autonomy, and freedom to make decisions.
  • A valued role with unique responsibilities and plenty of room for creativity.
  • Fast feedback and recognition of your contributions in a high-growth company.
  • Strong team spirit - successes are celebrated with company events and trips.


Practicalities

To apply, please send us your CV and, in lieu of providing a cover letter, concisely answer the questions on the application. If your profile looks like a good match, we will get in touch to coordinate an online technical test as a first step in the interview process.


Should you have questions about the role that you would like clarified before applying, you're welcome to send an email to quant_jobs@alipescapital.com. Please note that applications should not be sent to the email address but rather via the job link.

Title: AI Trainer - Freelance Data Annotator
Description:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.

At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence.

What We Do

The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.

About The Role

Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses - when projects are available.

Responsibilities:


Carefully review provided data (text, images, or videos)
Label or classify content based on project guidelines
Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear material


Important note: This is project-based work. Tasks are available only when projects are active. You may be invited to one or more projects depending on your profile and current opportunities.

Each project has its own compensation level based on scope and expertise required. On this project, AI trainers earn up to $22 per hour equivalent.

Requirements


Bachelor's degree in any discipline
Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role
Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities
Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions
Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed
Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence


Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?


Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments
Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio
Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise

Title: AI Innovation Lead
Description:
Build and lead an AI Lab from scratch for a WealthTech platform – and shape how AI transforms software, products, and decision-making




On behalf of our client Aleta, Bloom is looking for a technically visionary, hands-on AI Innovation Lead to build and lead a dedicated AI Lab in their HQ in central Aarhus, Denmark.




The company Aleta is on an ambitious journey to become a truly AI-first organization – not by adding AI on top, but by fundamentally rethinking how products are built, how software is written, and how decisions are made. This is a rare opportunity to work alongside highly skilled colleagues in an innovative environment and take a leading role in shaping the future of WealthTech.

At Aleta, the belief is that the future of technology development will look fundamentally different from today. This role exists to lead that transformation.




The ambition is clear: Aleta aims to become an AI-first organization, where AI is embedded intelligently across products, processes, and decision-making. To support this, a dedicated AI Lab is being established to explore, build, and operationalize new ways of working with AI – not as side experiments, but as a core driver of how the company operates.




The Aleta AI Lab




The AI Lab will be established as a dedicated, autonomous unit in Aarhus, close to – but intentionally distinct from – the core engineering organization.




The AI Lab will have:




A direct reporting line to the C-suite
A clear mandate to challenge existing assumptions, stacks, and workflows
Responsibility for translating frontier AI into real, scalable product value

Your mission and key responsibilities




As AI Innovation Lead, you will own the creation and evolution of Aleta’s AI Lab.

From day one, you will:




Build and lead a small, high-impact AI team, with the mandate to grow it over time
Set the direction for how AI is applied across product, engineering, and operations
Own the evolution of Aleta’s AI-first tech stack




Aleta operates from a set of strong beliefs:




The future of software development is closer to natural language than classical programming
Winning teams will master LLMs, agentic systems, orchestration, and human-in-the-loop design
The best AI stacks are pragmatic and outcome-driven – not tool-driven




Your task is not to chase trends, but to build what works.




What you will do




Build and operate the AI Lab as a high-impact delivery unit
Translate AI capabilities into tangible product and operational outcomes
Design, build, and evaluate AI-driven workflows, agents, copilots, and internal tools
Work hands-on with modern AI stacks (LLMs, embeddings, retrieval, orchestration)
Make architectural decisions in an evolving AI-native landscape
Collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, and Leadership to drive adoption
Continuously challenge how things are done today




Success in this role means moving AI from experimentation to measurable impact.




Who we are looking for

You combine curiosity about new technology with strong execution capabilities, and




Can turn vision into concrete projects and measurable outcomes
Have a strong technical understanding and are comfortable being hands-on
Move fluidly between strategic, tactical, and operational levels
Learn fast and thrive in high-expectation environments
Thrive in the face of ambiguity and incomplete information
Take high ownership and drive progress – speed over perfection, iterate fast
Rebuild rather than patch – chooses long-term quality and velocity over short-term fixes
Follows shared standards and works within a system – not personal style or ego




Strong fundamentals and a high learning velocity are key. You bring technical vision and hands-on execution in equal measure.




What Aleta offers




Aleta offers a rare opportunity to build an AI Lab from scratch and have direct influence on the future of a modern wealth platform. You will work closely with an ambitious, product-driven leadership team and have the freedom to design a best-in-class AI-first tech stack.

At the same time, Aleta provides a fast-moving and innovative environment where ideas are valued and quickly turned into action. You will join a team of highly skilled, ambitious, and supportive colleagues who take ownership and enjoy building impactful solutions together.

The culture is characterized by:




Innovation over bureaucracy – ideas are explored and tested, not parked
A strong social environment with events, collaboration, and knowledge sharing
An inspiring and modern office environment in Aarhus C
A balance between deep focus and creative sparring




More About Aleta




Aleta is a next-generation wealth platform built for forward-thinking family offices and wealth managers ready to move beyond legacy systems. The platform manages more than USD 100 billion in assets and supports complex ownership structures, reporting, and decision-making.

Headquartered in central Aarhus alongside its core development team, Aleta operates globally with offices in New York, Aarhus, and Bangkok – combining strong execution with an international outlook.




Interested in joining Aleta?




At Bloom, we are actively reviewing applications and look forward to receiving your Danish or English application and CV as soon as possible.




If you have any questions, please feel free to contact Dorte Pape at Bloom via dp@bloom.dk or +453160 9365




If you would like to be considered for the position, please apply through Bloom’s website using the following link: https://bloom.dk/jobs/ai-innovation-lead-393/

Title: Lead Data Scientist - Copenhagen
Description:
Do you want to research and build advanced analytics solutions using the latest tech? And are you genuinely excited by data-driven forecasting – a discipline that draws on classical data science, true statistical knowledge and strong engineering?

Join our Advanced Analytics & AI team to help improve and grow our data science portfolio with a strong focus on forecasting. As a structured, hands-on leader, you will help set the technical direction for the team. Making your mark on data science at ECCO, you will help empower business units to make data-driven decisions and automate operations.

Join our Data & AI tech hub in Copenhagen

We lead the data science initiatives and capabilities in the ECCO Group and drive an ambitious strategy to fundamentally improve how ECCO operates based on data. This means working with the entire value chain from logistics and sales to marketing, pricing and store operations.

As a Lead Data Scientist, You Will Develop, Implement And Deliver Complex Solutions, Ranging From Exploratory Analyses To Automated Solutions In Production. Using DevOps And DataOps Pipelines, You Will Deploy And Maintain Solutions, And You Will


Be a leading mind in the Data Science & Machine Learning product team, working closely together with skilled ML engineers and (Sr) Data Scientists
Lead the architectural vision and technical implementation of the team’s data science solutions, setting clear standards and structure for how we design, build and ship
Scope and develop new data science products with strong core in forecasting, but also expanding the team scope into other core data science disciplines over time
Deliver solutions based on a modern data platform that is delivered by a state-of-the-art Data Platform team, enabling more focus on data science, and less worry about data pipelines


You enjoy applying analytics to real-life business challenges

While self-driven and resourceful, you enjoy working in teams to solve challenges and develop the best solutions. You are genuinely interested in forecasting and enjoy the more classical data science and statistics disciplines it builds on. This curiosity matters more to us than years of forecasting experience.

You work in a structured way and like bringing clarity and sound architecture to complex problems. With your creative can-do mindset and strong problem-solving skills, you can find opportunities for process optimisation and test the effectiveness of different models and courses of action.

You Are Always Interested In Learning New Technologies And Ways Of Working – And Open To Unlearning Old Ones. Moreover, We Imagine That You


Hold a master’s degree or PhD in a quantitative discipline, e.g. statistics, mathematics, engineering or operation research
Have significant experience as a Senior Data Scientist or an equivalent quantitative role with a strong focus on statistics and the classical data science disciplines; hands-on forecasting experience is a plus but not a requirement
Have significant experience using data science/database software and programming languages, such as SQL, R and Python
Have experience with unified analytics engines such as Snowflake, as well as with container platform/orchestration systems such as Docker/Kubernetes
Are fluent in English and have solid communication skills; fluency in Danish is a plus.


Ideally you bring a software engineering background or strong engineering expertise or, alternatively, experience running machine learning systems in production at scale; either is a significant advantage.

We tackle an endless range of new opportunities

You can look forward to joining a tech hub with 30+ highly skilled, motivated, collaborative and fun-loving colleagues. We share an entrepreneurial spirit and a bias for action. With a broad mandate to experiment, explore and innovate, we are tackling an endless range of new opportunities.

We are always ready to implement new technologies into our workflow. In addition, your continued personal and professional development is essential to us, and we offer participation in internal and external courses, education and conferences supporting this development.

Are you ready for your next challenge?

We invite candidates for interviews on an ongoing basis, so please do not hesitate to apply. Applications are reviewed continuously. However, please note that we reserve the right to amend or withdraw vacancies at any time, including before the advertised closing date. So, if you are interested in this role, we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

For additional information, you can reach out to Head of Advanced Analytics & AI, Thomas Jauho THJAU@ecco.com.

Imagining yourself at ECCO? Get a glimpse of what your new exciting career entails on

CREATE THE FOOTPRINTS OF TOMORROW

Join us and you become part of a meaningful, developing and multicultural workplace with colleagues who are proud to create the footprints of tomorrow. Our offices and facilities around the world have an informal culture and relaxed dress code – our shoe code is slightly stricter.

Are you curious, passionate and ready to make an impact? Then you’ll fit right in.

Title: Applied AI Engineer
Description:
Tech-first. Ego-free. User-obsessed.




Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden to understand and optimise their taxes. We automate tax tracking, surface deductions people didn't know they had, and give users the insights they need to make better financial decisions — all directly connected to the Danish tax authority.




We're an independent company of around 50 people based in Copenhagen, with a 4.7-star rating and a simple mission: make financial insight accessible to everyone, not just those who can afford an accountant.




We have an ambitious multi-year vision for where we take the product next. This role helps build the conversational and agentic layer that makes it credible.







About the role




You'll work primarily on the LLM-powered parts of Skatteguiden. Today, that's a mix of customer-facing and internal products: RAG-based assistants and agentic systems, running on Azure infrastructure.




Over time, the agentic side of this stack will grow significantly, and the infrastructure needed to get there safely is part of the job.




This is a role about building agentic systems the right way — with quality, evaluations, and guardrails as first-class concerns. Alongside your team, you'll regularly partner with our Head of Security & Privacy and our Legal/Compliance team.




You'll join the AI Products team, working closely with our existing Data Scientist, our AI Engineer, and our incoming Senior ML Engineer. While we each have our specialisations and focus, we enjoy collaborating across our roles as our team is small and dynamic.







What you'll do




- Build on our existing LLM products. Ship improvements to quality, latency, cost, and safety. These are production systems used every day by users and internal teams.

- Build out the evaluation infrastructure. Offline evals, online validation, regression tracking, LLM-as-a-judge pipelines where they make sense. Evals aren't an afterthought at Skatteguiden; they drive our product decisions.

- Help define SLOs. Latency, quality, cost, and safety. Formal SLOs for production LLM systems are part of the bread and butter. You'll help define them.

- Contribute to the agentic trust infrastructure. Human-in-the-loop review flows, explicit consent, reversibility, and audit trails. As the product moves towards further maturity, this is what separates a product users trust from one they don't.

- Partner on red teaming and compliance. Work with Security & Privacy on adversarial testing and with Legal on regulatory fit.

- Stay on top of the model landscape. Azure today, but the landscape moves fast. You'll help us stay current and avoid lock-in.







Who we're looking for




You're an engineer who has built LLM systems and has opinions about how to do it well. You think evals are a first-class concern. You design for challenges like hallucinations and prompt injection. You know agentic systems are exciting, but don't ship themselves.




You've shipped LLM systems that real users touched, you take evaluation seriously as an engineering discipline, and you have the instinct to know when something is working and when it isn't. If that's you, we'd like to hear from you.




This role spans more than just LLM work. Evaluation infrastructure, integration, guardrails, and the critical engineering around production AI systems are a big part of the job.







Must-have




- Working experience building LLM-powered applications in production. Not a weekend demo — something real users have touched.

- Hands-on grasp of RAG, tool use, and at least one orchestration framework (LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude Agent SDK, or equivalent).

- Experience with LLM evaluation; offline evals, online validation, or regression tracking. You treat quality as an engineering discipline, not a gut check.

- Solid software engineering foundations. Python, clean code, testing, CI/CD.

- Security and privacy awareness. You understand why guardrails matter for a product that handles financial data.

- Good judgment under ambiguity. LLM work is full of unclear trade-offs; you can reason about them and pick a direction.

- Genuine enthusiasm for AI. You use AI tools daily in your own work, and you're always looking for new ways to apply them.







Nice-to-have




- Experience with production agentic systems — tool use, planning, human-in-the-loop review flows.

- Experience with Azure OpenAI and Azure AI Search.

- Experience with Agent-to-Agent (A2A) or similar protocols

- Prior work in fintech, healthtech, or another regulated domain.

- Familiarity with LLM observability tooling.

- Danish language is useful but not required. The team works in English.







Why join us




- A vision with real ambition. From conversational guidance today to an AI Companion that helps users plan and eventually act on their behalf. You'll help build the layer that makes it safe and credible.

- Quality and evals as a first-class discipline. Not a checkbox. Not a sprint goal. A permanent part of the job, with real backing from leadership.

- Room to grow. Mid-level today, but the scope of the work and the team's growth mean you'll have real room to step up. This is a good role to grow in seniority.

- The product matters. Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden. Your work keeps it trustworthy.

- Solid company. Independent, profitable, growing. No VC pressure, no bloated roadmap. Just a clear mission and a team that cares.

- A company that's serious about AI. We use AI across the business, and we're only going deeper. Being a small, focused team means we can move fast on this — no lengthy approval chains, no slow rollouts.

- Good people. We take the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously. The culture is professional, informal, and genuinely collaborative.







Practical details




Location: Copenhagen. We're an office-first company, and it's a deliberate choice. We're moving fast, we have big ambitions, and we genuinely believe that being physically together is one of the things that makes that possible. Things move quicker when you can turn your chair and talk to someone.




We're a five-minute walk from Rådhuspladsen, stocked with everything from fruit to fizzy drinks to afternoon snacks, and lunch is at Claus Meyer's canteen just across the street — proper food, not an afterthought. If you're putting in a long day, dinner is covered too.




Start: As soon as possible.




Salary: Competitive and based on your experience.







Our lean hiring process




1. Screening call with our recruiter (30 min) ONLINE

2. Technical interview with the Head of AI Products and an AI engineer (120 min) ONSITE

3. Case / technical deep-dive with the AI Products team (60 min) ONSITE

4. Final conversation with the CTO and CEO (30 min) ONSITE

HTML:
Tech-first. Ego-free. User-obsessed.


Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden to understand and optimise their taxes. We automate tax tracking, surface deductions people didn't know they had, and give users the insights they need to make better financial decisions — all directly connected to the Danish tax authority.


We're an independent company of around 50 people based in Copenhagen, with a 4.7-star rating and a simple mission: make financial insight accessible to everyone, not just those who can afford an accountant.


We have an ambitious multi-year vision for where we take the product next. This role helps build the conversational and agentic layer that makes it credible.



About the role


You'll work primarily on the LLM-powered parts of Skatteguiden. Today, that's a mix of customer-facing and internal products: RAG-based assistants and agentic systems, running on Azure infrastructure.


Over time, the agentic side of this stack will grow significantly, and the infrastructure needed to get there safely is part of the job.


This is a role about building agentic systems the right way — with quality, evaluations, and guardrails as first-class concerns. Alongside your team, you'll regularly partner with our Head of Security & Privacy and our Legal/Compliance team.


You'll join the AI Products team, working closely with our existing Data Scientist, our AI Engineer, and our incoming Senior ML Engineer. While we each have our specialisations and focus, we enjoy collaborating across our roles as our team is small and dynamic.



What you'll do


- Build on our existing LLM products. Ship improvements to quality, latency, cost, and safety. These are production systems used every day by users and internal teams.

- Build out the evaluation infrastructure. Offline evals, online validation, regression tracking, LLM-as-a-judge pipelines where they make sense. Evals aren't an afterthought at Skatteguiden; they drive our product decisions.

- Help define SLOs. Latency, quality, cost, and safety. Formal SLOs for production LLM systems are part of the bread and butter. You'll help define them.

- Contribute to the agentic trust infrastructure. Human-in-the-loop review flows, explicit consent, reversibility, and audit trails. As the product moves towards further maturity, this is what separates a product users trust from one they don't.

- Partner on red teaming and compliance. Work with Security & Privacy on adversarial testing and with Legal on regulatory fit.

- Stay on top of the model landscape. Azure today, but the landscape moves fast. You'll help us stay current and avoid lock-in.



Who we're looking for


You're an engineer who has built LLM systems and has opinions about how to do it well. You think evals are a first-class concern. You design for challenges like hallucinations and prompt injection. You know agentic systems are exciting, but don't ship themselves.


You've shipped LLM systems that real users touched, you take evaluation seriously as an engineering discipline, and you have the instinct to know when something is working and when it isn't. If that's you, we'd like to hear from you.


This role spans more than just LLM work. Evaluation infrastructure, integration, guardrails, and the critical engineering around production AI systems are a big part of the job.



Must-have


- Working experience building LLM-powered applications in production. Not a weekend demo — something real users have touched.

- Hands-on grasp of RAG, tool use, and at least one orchestration framework (LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude Agent SDK, or equivalent).

- Experience with LLM evaluation; offline evals, online validation, or regression tracking. You treat quality as an engineering discipline, not a gut check.

- Solid software engineering foundations. Python, clean code, testing, CI/CD.

- Security and privacy awareness. You understand why guardrails matter for a product that handles financial data.

- Good judgment under ambiguity. LLM work is full of unclear trade-offs; you can reason about them and pick a direction.

- Genuine enthusiasm for AI. You use AI tools daily in your own work, and you're always looking for new ways to apply them.



Nice-to-have


- Experience with production agentic systems — tool use, planning, human-in-the-loop review flows.

- Experience with Azure OpenAI and Azure AI Search.

- Experience with Agent-to-Agent (A2A) or similar protocols

- Prior work in fintech, healthtech, or another regulated domain.

- Familiarity with LLM observability tooling.

- Danish language is useful but not required. The team works in English.



Why join us


- A vision with real ambition. From conversational guidance today to an AI Companion that helps users plan and eventually act on their behalf. You'll help build the layer that makes it safe and credible.

- Quality and evals as a first-class discipline. Not a checkbox. Not a sprint goal. A permanent part of the job, with real backing from leadership.

- Room to grow. Mid-level today, but the scope of the work and the team's growth mean you'll have real room to step up. This is a good role to grow in seniority.

- The product matters. Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden. Your work keeps it trustworthy.

- Solid company. Independent, profitable, growing. No VC pressure, no bloated roadmap. Just a clear mission and a team that cares.

- A company that's serious about AI. We use AI across the business, and we're only going deeper. Being a small, focused team means we can move fast on this — no lengthy approval chains, no slow rollouts.

- Good people. We take the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously. The culture is professional, informal, and genuinely collaborative.



Practical details


Location: Copenhagen. We're an office-first company, and it's a deliberate choice. We're moving fast, we have big ambitions, and we genuinely believe that being physically together is one of the things that makes that possible. Things move quicker when you can turn your chair and talk to someone.


We're a five-minute walk from Rådhuspladsen, stocked with everything from fruit to fizzy drinks to afternoon snacks, and lunch is at Claus Meyer's canteen just across the street — proper food, not an afterthought. If you're putting in a long day, dinner is covered too.


Start: As soon as possible.


Salary: Competitive and based on your experience.



Our lean hiring process


1. Screening call with our recruiter (30 min) ONLINE

2. Technical interview with the Head of AI Products and an AI engineer (120 min) ONSITE

3. Case / technical deep-dive with the AI Products team (60 min) ONSITE

4. Final conversation with the CTO and CEO (30 min) ONSITE

Title: Student Worker - Machine Learning Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Veo is a global leader in AI-based sports camera technology. Our innovative, fully automatic camera solution enables sports teams to record matches and training sessions without a camera operator. We’re democratizing the world of sports by granting video analysis for teams on all levels—a privilege that used to be only for the few. More than 40,000 clubs in 90+ countries record their games every week.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We’re a diverse group of innovative thinkers, creators, and problem-solvers who believe in delivering an incredible product—and having fun while doing it.

The Opportunity

As a Student Machine Learning Engineer at Veo you will get to work on challenging problems and directly contribute to the value created by our AI-driven end-user products.

You will become part of our AI team, which comprises 12 researchers and engineers that are responsible for all stages in the machine learning lifecycles across different projects - from scoping and defining data annotation tasks to modeling, validation, and deployment. You will work on well-defined tasks that align with the team’s goals, getting guidance and sparring from experienced teammates to the extent you need.

We stay current with the latest research and continuously discuss concepts and ideas in recent papers to assess their relevance to our tasks and product-specific challenges.

We are looking to add an ambitious, high-performing student worker with a strong academic record, experience with multiple machine learning projects, good coding skills, and a passion for the field.

What You Will Do:



Train state-of-the-art machine learning models
Write efficient inference pipelines for cloud and edge
Define and supervise data annotation tasks
Improve evaluation schemes to increase understanding of model performance and shortcomings




What You Could Bring:



BSc/MSc ongoing within a relevant field and with a strong academic record
Ability to work onsite from the Copenhagen office 1 or 2 days per week
Hands-on experience with multiple machine-learning projects in Python and PyTorch
Good coding skills
Ability to work both independently and as part of a team




Our Copenhagen office mirrors our innovative technology – with an indoor ball court, rooftop terrace, and well-equipped gym. We have created a facility that supports connecting disciplines across the business, facilitates creative thinking, and gives the space to engage with colleagues, our global partners, and the entire Veo community.

Values we hold at Veo:

Dare - We take initiative, stay curious, and challenge the status quo in our work.

Own it - We drive impact by identifying opportunities, taking responsibility, and confidently executing.

Level up - We embrace growth, continuously learning and shaping our skills.

Play as a team - We collaborate across a diverse organization, leveraging shared insights for stronger outcomes.

Customer Centric - We deliver value by having close partnerships and a deep understanding of our customers (internally and externally)

We value diversity and inclusion and welcome applicants from all backgrounds, even if your experience doesn’t completely match every qualification listed. What matters most is your creativity, potential, and ability to contribute meaningfully.

Title: Open Talent Pool - all DHI locations
Place: Hørsholm, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At DHI, water, environment and sustainability meet innovation. Join us in enabling a sustainable future for water!

Looking to make an impact on our planet? At DHI, you can. We work with water in all its forms – combining deep domain knowledge, advanced digital technology, and more than 60 years of research and innovation to help society use, manage and live with water sustainably. With over 1,100 engineers and specialists across 26 countries, we are continually expanding our global community of experts.

As we grow, new opportunities emerge throughout the year. Even when a specific vacancy isn’t yet published, we welcome proactive applications from individuals who want to contribute to our mission – including senior specialist with years of experience or students, recent graduates and early career professionals who are excited to build their future in the water sector.

Why submit an open application?

Our business evolves quickly, and new roles – technical, scientific, digital and business-oriented – are created as projects grow and priorities shift. Submitting an open application adds you to our Open Talent Pool, enabling us to reach out when a suitable opportunity arises, often before it is advertised.

Before you submit your open application, please make sure to check our current vacancies as well.

Opportunities for Students & Early Career Talent

We strongly encourage students and early career professionals to apply.

At DHI, You Can


Join as an intern, student assistant, thesis collaboration partner or industry master program student
Contribute to global water projects alongside experienced specialists
Apply your academic knowledge to real-world environmental and digital challenges
Grow your skills in engineering, modelling, climate resilience, data science, software development, sustainability and more
Work in a culture that supports experimentation, learning and exploration


If you are studying or recently graduated in engineering, environmental science, climate studies, digital technology, or related fields – and want to help shape a sustainable future – your application is very welcome.

Profiles we are interested in

We invite applications from experienced professionals, graduates, and students with interest or expertise in:


Engineering & Environmental Science Coastal, marine, environmental, hydraulic, climate, civil, ecosystem modelling
Data, Digital & Technology Software development, data science, machine learning, AI, digital product development
Consulting & Project Delivery Advisory services, modelling, client engagement, project/programme management
Sustainability & ESG
Corporate Functions Legal, compliance, HR, finance, operations, commercial roles


We value cross disciplinary thinkers—if your background doesn’t fit into a category, we still encourage you to apply.

What We Offer

When you join DHI, you are empowered to be an explorer—to innovate, collaborate, and continuously learn. You’ll be part of a global organisation committed to protecting water ecosystems and developing sustainable solutions for communities, cities, industries and the planet.

Positions May Be


Fulltime or parttime
Based in various global locations or open to flexible arrangements
Project based or permanent, depending on organisational needs


How To Apply


Please submit your application through our careers page under “Open Talent”. If you are interested in other locations, please indicate that clearly.


Kindly Attach


Your CV
A brief note outlining your area of interests, skills and how you found DHI
List your preferred DHI location(s)
Links to portfolio, publications, or project work (optional)


Learn more about DHI www.dhigroup.com
Show more

Title: Research Scientist
Place: Birkerød, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join us to Make an Impact

Innovation is in our DNA. We invented supercontinuum lasers and photonic crystal fibers and we constantly reimagine how our products can be used and improved. Our customers use our products in medical and life science research, quantum computing and space exploration.

We seek curious, creative and collaborative people to work in our cutting-edge company. Can you see yourself working in teams and providing solutions for innovators?

If you wish to apply, please contact us with your CV / Résumé. If you have a cover letter, please save this in one document with your CV / Résumé as you are only able to upload one document.

We look forward to hearing from you!

THE ROLE

You will be joining our highly skilled Future Technologies, Optical Engineering team to develop solutions for new laser systems and research projects. You will be working on various laser and optoelectronics technologies, utilising elements of NKTP's existing products and evaluating new technologies to incorporate into NKTP's technology roadmap. Main responsibilities may include but not limited to General Product/Process Research Science and is focused on scientific research, development, and testing of new products, processes, and raw materials .The daily work may be in close collaboration with the other departments across optical, electronic, and mechanical teams, as well as with external project partners from industry and academia.

Personal Skills We Seek


Be responsible for own work and self-driven in daily work
Enjoy working closely with experts in optics, electronics, and mechanical for design and problem solving
Able to take ownership of project work packages ensuring timely delivery and reporting when needed


Relevant technical skills


PhD in Optics or similar preferred
Laser design theory - ideally in fiber
Experience with laser feedback and control
Laser lab experience - e.g. free-space optics alignment, testing and measurement
Optical system modelling - e.g. split-step GNLSE, rate-equation-based amplifier modelling
Familiarity with Python, Matlab, and MS Office tools
English (written and spoken)


WE OFFER

Professional challenges in a highly dynamic and growing business environment with enormous potential for further growth. An opportunity to become part of a successful, enthusiastic, development team in an informal workplace. Competitive employment terms and benefits, good work-life balance, and influence on own tasks. Experienced and skilled colleagues with highly diverse backgrounds in an international organization.

APPLY TODAY

We plan to invite candidates for interviews as soon as possible. If you think you have what it takes, please send your CV to us and we will be in contact soon.

Keep your personal data safe

NKT Photonics ("The Company") take responsibly for the protection of your personal data. Accordingly, upon receiving your job application and additional material you choose to submit, the Company keeps a safe and secure environment and ensures confidentiality.

You also have a role to play in keeping your personal data safe. We request you only submit personal data about yourself in your job application, CV / Résumé or other relevant material you choose to provide to us that is relevant for the specific job opening you are applying for.

Please consider whether there is personal information you are about to submit, such as health information, information on family relations, information on trade union membership, etc. that is not necessary for our recruitment process or the job opening you are applying for. Please also be aware that certificates or other documents are not necessary for the vacancy. We urgently request you to redact/cross out such information before you submit your job application.

Thank you.
Show more

Title: Senior Python Data Scraping Engineer (Freelance)
Description:
Mindrift is looking for highly skilled Senior Python Data Scraping Engineers to join the Tendem project and drive specialized data scraping workflows within our hybrid AI + human system.

In this role, as an AI Pilot - that's how we refer to this role at Mindrift - you'll collaborate with Tendem Agents that handle repetitive tasks, while you provide critical thinking, domain expertise, and quality control to deliver accurate and actionable results.

This part-time remote opportunity is ideal for technical professionals with hands-on experience in web scraping, data extraction and processing.

What We Do

The Mindrift platform connects specialists with AI projects from major tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the potential of Generative AI by tapping into real-world expertise from across the globe.

About The Role

This is a freelance role for a Tendem project. As a Senior Python Data Scraping Engineer, you'll handle data scraping tasks requiring technical precision for web extraction and processing, utilizing various tools such as our provided Apify and OpenRouter alongside your own resourceful approaches.

Key Responsibilities


Own end-to-end data extraction workflows across complex websites, ensuring complete coverage, accuracy, and reliable delivery of structured datasets.
Leverage internal tools (Apify, OpenRouter) alongside custom workflows to accelerate data collection, validation, and task execution while meeting defined requirements.
Ensure reliable extraction from dynamic and interactive web sources, adapting approaches as needed to handle JavaScript-rendered content and changing site behavior.
Enforce data quality standards through validation checks, cross-source consistency controls, adherence to formatting specifications, and systematic verification prior to delivery.
Scale scraping operations for large datasets using efficient batching or parallelization, monitor failures, and maintain stability against minor site structure changes


Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $37 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Requirements


At least 5 year of relevant experience in data engineering, web scraping, automation, or software development (required)
Bachelor's or Master's Degree in Engineering, Applied Mathematics, Computer Science, or related technical fields is a plus
Strong experience in Python web scraping (BeautifulSoup, Selenium or similar), including dynamic content (JS, AJAX, infinite scroll) and APIs via proxies
Proven ability to extract data from complex structures (hierarchies, archived pages, inconsistent HTML)
Solid background in data cleaning, normalization, and validation, delivering structured datasets (CSV, JSON, Google Sheets)
Demonstrated experience handling anti-bot mechanisms and dynamic site structures at scale
Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS or equivalent) and containerization (Docker) as part of real workflows
Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks (LangChain, OpenRouter, or similar) applied to automation tasks
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Self-directed work ethic with ability to troubleshoot independently
A link to GitHub is a plus
English proficiency: Upper-intermediate (B2) or above (required)


Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?


Work fully remote on your own schedule with just a laptop and stable internet connection.
Gain hands-on experience in a unique hybrid environment where human expertise and AI agents collaborate seamlessly — a distinctive skill set in a rapidly growing field.
Participate in performance-based bonus programs that reward high-quality work and consistent delivery
Show more

Title: Scientific Software Developer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Scientific Software Developer

Join the Carlsberg Research Laboratory (CRL) in Copenhagen and help us build the software that accelerates scientific discovery. We are rethinking how research is done by embedding software and AI directly into real-world scientific workflows across domains such as chemistry, bioinformatics, sensory science, and machine learning.

What you’ll be doing

You will design and build the systems that power modern research, from data pipelines to AI-enabled tools. You won’t just prototype ideas. You’ll turn them into reliable, production-grade software used by scientists every day.


Build and own end-to-end scientific software systems
Develop automation that removes manual research workflows
Integrate modern AI capabilities into real-world applications
Work closely with domain experts across sensory science, chemistry, and bioinformatics to translate research needs into scalable software
Translate complex scientific problems into clean, scalable technical solutions
Ship quickly, iterate, and continuously improve


What we’re looking for


Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field, or equivalent experience; a PhD is a plus
Strong foundation in software engineering and system design
Experience building scalable systems, APIs, and data pipelines
Familiarity with AI/ML systems and modern AI tools and SDKs


You:


You have experience building systems from scratch
Are comfortable working at the intersection of software and real-world experimentation
Communicate effectively with both engineers and domain experts
Are excited to learn new scientific domains
You are comfortable working in evolving problem spaces


What you can expect

You will join a multidisciplinary team working at the intersection of software, AI, and science. You will build systems that reduce manual work, improve reproducibility, and accelerate how quickly research turns into real-world impact. You will have the opportunity to collaborate with domain specialists in the world of bioinformatics, sensory science, chemistry, and machine learning.

You can expect a fixed-term contract for three years.

Interested?

Apply through the link. Please notice that this is the only way we accept applications.

To help ensure a smooth recruitment process, we kindly ask that all application documents be submitted in English.

You do not need to attach a cover letter to your application, but please include a few sentences about why you are applying in your resume or CV.

The deadline for the application is the 26th of May. We read applications continuously, and vacancies may be filled sooner than the deadline, so we encourage you to apply as early as possible.

We look forward to receiving your application.

Title: Senior Rust Developer
Description:
Senior Rust Developer – Core Tech




We’re looking for a senior Rust developer to join our Core Tech team and help us scale the engine that powers SASHA’s image and video protection technology.




You'll move across the codebase: image and video pipelines, ML inference, cross-platform bindings, performance work, and the tooling that ships it all. We care more about depth in Rust and the ability to take ownership of unfamiliar problems than about any specific domain background.   




About SASHA

SASHA is a privately funded deep-tech start-up on a mission to make it safe to share media online. Our technology embeds an invisible, distribution-resilient signature into each distributed copy of a photo or video – designed to survive the transformations real-world sharing applies (compression, resizing, format conversion, screenshots) so that, in principle, a leaked or misused copy can be traced back to a specific point in the distribution chain. Making that work reliably at scale is what we're building. 




The ideal candidate

Senior-level Rust experience, ideally with the async ecosystem and FFI work
Strong systems fundamentals: performance, memory, concurrency, cross-platform builds
Comfortable across the stack – happy to dig into image formats, ONNX inference, mobile bindings and compile time security
A higher education in computer science, engineering, or similar – or equivalent demonstrable experience.
Enjoys sparring with teammates and communicating clearly about problems and solutions.
Comfortable working in a small team where priorities can change quickly.




Nice to have

Image and video processing, knowledge about image and video formats
Experience with cross-platform bindings or shipping SDKs in multiple languages
ML inference experience
C++ background

﻿

Why join us

Solve hard problems in image, video and digital content protection.
Work on a technology with real-world impact.
Join a small, talented, distributed team, where engineers have meaningful ownership
Start-up culture, where craft, curiosity and shipping are valued
Competitive salary




Location

We have offices in Odense and Copenhagen, Denmark. We’re open to EU residents who would be willing to relocate.

Title: Junior Unity Developer & XR tester (Part time student job)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you interested in helping develop and test digital solutions that can make a difference and help save lives?




Come join the Strategic Research team at Laerdal Copenhagen!




THE JOB

As our new Junior Unity Developer and XR tester, you will become part of our Strategic Research team, focusing on XR Production.

The Laerdal Strategic Research (SR) team is a diverse and experienced group specializing in research, development, and international collaboration.




The SR team focuses on scaling lifesaving impact by supporting lifesavers through better training, equipment, and system. Through generating evidence and supporting evidenced solutions, the SR team directly supports Laerdal’s overarching mission of Helping Save Lives.




The SR team connects science and technology to real-world impact by maintaining close, ethical collaboration with global research partners and key opinion leaders through collaborative research and meaningful innovation that advance lifesaving solutions.




The SR team works in an iterative, exploratory way - building, testing, learning, and refining ideas together with internal and external partners.




In this position, you will become part of our office in Copenhagen, and work closely with colleagues, stakeholders, and partners across Laerdal. You should expect ongoing collaboration with the SR team in Stavanger, Norway, as well as some travel. This may include:

Demos and prototype evaluations
Partner and stakeholder meetings
Conferences and events
Occasional visits to Laerdal’s headquarter in Stavanger







Your day-to-day responsibilities will be:




Prototype Development

Assist in the design and development of prototypes using Unity under the supervision of senior team members.
Help set up XR scenarios, implement basic functionality, and prepare prototype builds.
Provide feedback and suggestions to improve prototypes and user experience.




Testing & Quality Assurance

Execute manual test procedures thoroughly and accurately report, track, and verify defects.
Reproduce, investigate, and re-test incidents reported by customers or partners.
Contribute to usability, accessibility, performance, and security evaluation of prototypes.




Hardware, Headsets & Build Management

Set up and maintain XR headsets and related hardware.
Upload, manage, and maintain prototype builds and management software.
Ensure devices and tools are updated and functioning correctly.




Collaboration & Processes

Participate in meetings and research processes.
Perform or request code/design reviews and seek guidance when needed.
Assist with tasks outside primary discipline when relevant.
Update documentation, confluence pages, and user manuals.
Communicate challenges proactively with team and stakeholders.




You are...

Passionate and curious about new technology.
Currently pursuing studies in Computer Science or a closely related discipline.
Familiar with testing approaches and concepts.
Proficient with C# or other object-oriented programming languages
Knowledgeable of game engines such as Unity
Detail oriented and structured
Proactive, self-motivated and have a can-do attitude.
You are motivated by Laerdal’s mission of helping save lives.




It would be desirable if you:

are familiar with Git (or other version control systems)
have basic understanding of UI and UX principles.
XR knowledge is a plus but not required.
3D software knowledge is a plus but not required




Your Availability

Please, note that your application will not be considered unless:

You have at least 3 semesters left on your longest planned education.
You can work at least 12 hours a week.




Interested?

Apply with your CV and a short motivation explaining why this role speaks to you. (Remember, LinkedIn allows only one document, so please merge your CV and motivation letter into one file.)




If you have any questions that might keep you from applying, please reach out by e-mail to recruiting manager Bo Søhøj on bo.sohoj@laerdal.com




We’ll review applications and set up interviews on an ongoing basis.




THE COMPANY

Laerdal Copenhagen is the center of excellence for Laerdal's digital learning solutions. We provide best-in-class digital learning products, helping doctors and nurses around the world save lives.

With a unique mix of talents - ranging from medical doctors and user-experience designers to software engineers and talents from the video game industry - we work with international partners to deliver cutting-edge digital learning solutions that help save lives. We offer a collaborative and dynamic workplace characterized by a can-do attitude, an appreciation of diversity, flexible working conditions, and a casual atmosphere in an old warehouse in the center of Copenhagen.

You can get a feel of who we are in this video: https://vimeo.com/917084721/73a5d9e58a

Title: Graduate Program Data Science & Analytics - Denmark
Place: Ballerup Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you looking to kick off your career journey?

Do you like the idea of joining the European PayTech?

If so, you are at the right place!

Our Graduate Program offers you the chance to create your unique career path within our diverse international team, operating in more than 25 countries. Rotate among different departments, learn from experienced professionals, and make a difference by working on real business cases.

About The Program

Our Graduate Program is more than a learning path, it's a journey where you will work on real business cases and have a direct impact on business results.

What Can You Expect?


Career Path: Start with a permanent contract from day one. Over 12 months, experience two rotations across departments and transition into a landing role where you can continue to grow.
Training & Development: Receive a well-structured, dedicated Project Management training program, tailored to strengthen your ability to lead projects and deliver results. Plus, gain access to a wide range of learning opportunities through our Digital PayTech University, empowering you to continuously build your skills and expand your knowledge.
Impact & Networking: Contribute to real business solutions and expand your network by working alongside talented colleagues and senior leaders from across Europe.


Who are We Looking For?

If this sounds like you, we encourage you to apply:


You have graduated within the past 12 months or will graduate within the next 6 months with a master’s degree in Engineering, STEM disciplines, Economics, Business, Law, or related fields, and are available to work full-time.
You are fluent in English and Danish.
You bring international experience, whether through study, work abroad, or collaboration in a global environment.
You are a career starter with no more than one year of professional experience.
You have the legal right to work in Denmark (e.g., residency, visa).
You are committed, curious, and eager to take ownership of projects while thriving in a team environment.
You see formal training as just the beginning and aim to make a difference in everything you do.


Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

We are committed to building a diverse culture where talents from all walks of life feel they truly belong. By applying to our Graduate Program, you are one step closer to joining us. Next starts with you, everyday.

Apply Now!

Don't miss out on this amazing opportunity. Apply today and discover what's in it for you!

Please ensure both your CV and all answers in the application form are completed in English.

About Us

Are you interested in joining one of Europe's leading PayTech companies? Do you want to be part of a diverse team, collaborating with colleagues from across Europe? If so, you're in the right place!

About The Team

We are the result of merging Nexi, Nets, and Sia, with a presence in over 25 countries. With over 80 years of experience, we offer plenty of opportunities for personal and professional growth

At Nexi, we learn, adapt, and think like entrepreneurs. Discover more about our culture by visiting this page: https://www.nexigroup.com/en/people/life-at-nexi/overview/?utm_source=career-platform&utm_medium=job-openings&utm_content=about-the-team

Title: AI Deployment Engineer
Description:
AI Deployment Engineer – Smart Manufacturing AI Systems

Hybrid (Copenhagen, Denmark)

AI | MLOps | Industrial AI | Smart Manufacturing




About the Role

We’re looking for an AI Deployment Engineer to support the rollout of AI-driven systems used in smart manufacturing and industrial automation environments.




You’ll work on deploying and maintaining machine learning systems that support:

predictive maintenance
production optimisation
quality inspection
and operational intelligence across connected industrial platforms.




This role is ideal for engineers who enjoy working at the intersection of:

AI systems
cloud infrastructure
DevOps
and large-scale operational environments.




What You’ll Be Working On

Production deployment of AI systems supporting industrial automation workflows
Infrastructure for real-time monitoring and predictive analytics
Scalable AI rollout across connected manufacturing environments
CI/CD pipelines for machine learning and operational AI systems




Key Responsibilities

Deploy and manage machine learning models in production environments
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for AI workflows
Containerise and orchestrate services using Docker and Kubernetes
Monitor model performance, reliability, and system health
Collaborate with AI engineers and platform teams to optimise deployment strategies
Support model lifecycle management, rollback strategies, and observability
Ensure scalability and resilience of AI infrastructure in operational environments




Required Skills & Experience

3+ years experience in DevOps, MLOps, or cloud infrastructure engineering
Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines and automation
Hands-on experience with Docker and containerised environments
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Familiarity with machine learning deployment workflows
Understanding of scalable distributed systems and infrastructure reliability




Nice to Have

Experience with Kubernetes or orchestration platforms
Exposure to industrial systems, IoT, or manufacturing environments
Familiarity with monitoring and observability tooling
Experience supporting real-time AI inference systems




Why Join?

Work on real-world AI systems driving industrial transformation
Hybrid flexibility in Copenhagen
High-impact role in a growing Industrial AI / Industry 4.0 environment
Opportunity to contribute to large-scale AI adoption in manufacturing




If you’re passionate about deploying scalable AI systems into real operational environments, we’d love to hear from you.




Apply via XpertDirect — connecting companies with advanced AI infrastructure and deployment specialists.

Title: Engineering Internship
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Shure Scandinavia, we offer internships within different engineering disciplines and we are always on the outlook for talented students, who are passionate about their field of study.

As an intern at Shure, you will be part of the professional team of developers who are passionate about technologies, innovation and product quality. We are eager to share knowledge and to learn from each other - both locally and with our colleagues in the US, India, Scotland and China.

Inclusion, trust and respect for one another is at the core of our robust company culture, and we have a dynamic and open work environment with lots of opportunities for learning and development. Our workplace is inspiring, flexible, and informal with ample possibilities for learning and growth. Shure is an equal opportunity employer, and we celebrate diversity and cherish inclusion in the workplace.

Responsibilities

Embedded Software

If you have a passion for embedded SW development using C, C++, and Python, you’ll thrive here. We offer dynamic, high‑impact assignments where you’ll play a key role in designing and implementing innovative product features and solutions. You’ll get hands‑on experience across every layer of product development, from task estimation and architecture, all the way to implementation and integration, shaping technology that truly makes a difference.

Application Software

Are you into working with HTML5, CSS, Typescript and frameworks such as Angular and Playwright for testing? Then you can contribute to the development of great functionalities within Shure’s growing portfolio of software applications for desktop and cloud interacting with state of the art Shure hardware products

Digital Signal Processing

In Signal Processing and Applied Research, you will be given assignments within design, simulation, development, and test of real time audio signal processing algorithms for various HW/SW platform/systems that are used on our wide suite of ‘Sound Extraordinary’ products. This also covers the field of adopting AI/Machine Learning techniques for getting superior audio solutions out to our customers.

In

FPGA

An internship within FPGA and Integrated Circuits Design will include design and coordination of FPGA circuits with use of VHDL for implementation of core signal processing and control elements as well as interfaces to other hardware. In addition hereto, you will be doing circuits test and analysis.

DevOps

In DevOps,we help teams deliver firmware, software, and data by automating repetitove tasks and steamlining our build set-up.

We use Jenkins, Git(Bitbucket), Python 3.12+, Linux and Docker as well AWS RDS and Windows, and we visualize data using Grafana that enables our colleagues to make data driven decisions. In

System Verification

You'll enhance automated testing, collaborate on key metrics, design and execute tests, and analyze results.

Gain hands-on experience in system verification and quality improvement, contributing to our innovative audio solutions.

Qualifications

Your profile and application:

As a person, you are focused and have an eye for detail. You see opportunities in the challenges that occur during your work day you and you enjoy collaborating with team members from different parts of the world. Shure is a global company and we have many talented developers who are always ready to review and discuss new ideas, methods and solutions. Ignite the pure power of engineering: Join Shure and have a great internship.

At Shure, in addition to practical experience, you receive salary during your internship.

When you apply, please tell us why you are interested in an Internship at Shure and remember to include an overview of the courses - including grades - you have completed. Pleae also include information on when you expect to start your internship according to your studies.
