At Shure Scandinavia, we offer internships within different engineering disciplines and we are always on the outlook for talented students, who are passionate about their field of study.

As an intern at Shure, you will be part of the professional team of developers who are passionate about technologies, innovation and product quality. We are eager to share knowledge and to learn from each other - both locally and with our colleagues in the US, India, Scotland and China.

Inclusion, trust and respect for one another is at the core of our robust company culture, and we have a dynamic and open work environment with lots of opportunities for learning and development. Our workplace is inspiring, flexible, and informal with ample possibilities for learning and growth. Shure is an equal opportunity employer, and we celebrate diversity and cherish inclusion in the workplace.

Responsibilities

 Embedded Software

If you have a passion for embedded SW development using C, C++, and Python, you’ll thrive here. We offer dynamic, high‑impact assignments where you’ll play a key role in designing and implementing innovative product features and solutions. You’ll get hands‑on experience across every layer of product development, from task estimation and architecture, all the way to implementation and integration, shaping technology that truly makes a difference.

Application Software

Are you into working with HTML5, CSS, Typescript and frameworks such as Angular and Playwright for testing? Then you can contribute to the development of great functionalities within Shure’s growing portfolio of software applications for desktop and cloud interacting with state of the art Shure hardware products

Digital Signal Processing

In Signal Processing and Applied Research, you will be given assignments within design, simulation, development, and test of real time audio signal processing algorithms for various HW/SW platform/systems that are used on our wide suite of ‘Sound Extraordinary’ products. This also covers the field of adopting AI/Machine Learning techniques for getting superior audio solutions out to our customers.

In

FPGA

An internship within FPGA and Integrated Circuits Design will include design and coordination of FPGA circuits with use of VHDL for implementation of core signal processing and control elements as well as interfaces to other hardware. In addition hereto, you will be doing circuits test and analysis.

DevOps

In DevOps,we help teams deliver firmware, software, and data by automating repetitove tasks and steamlining our build set-up.

We use Jenkins, Git(Bitbucket), Python 3.12+, Linux and Docker as well AWS RDS and Windows, and we visualize data using Grafana that enables our colleagues to make data driven decisions. In

System Verification

You'll enhance automated testing, collaborate on key metrics, design and execute tests, and analyze results.

Gain hands-on experience in system verification and quality improvement, contributing to our innovative audio solutions.

Qualifications

Your profile and application:

As a person, you are focused and have an eye for detail. You see opportunities in the challenges that occur during your work day you and you enjoy collaborating with team members from different parts of the world. Shure is a global company and we have many talented developers who are always ready to review and discuss new ideas, methods and solutions. Ignite the pure power of engineering: Join Shure and have a great internship.

At Shure, in addition to practical experience, you receive salary during your internship.

When you apply, please tell us why you are interested in an Internship at Shure and remember to include an overview of the courses - including grades - you have completed. Pleae also include information on when you expect to start your internship according to your studies.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.

Your Responsibilities

Pipeline & Ingestion


Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.


Data Platform & Infrastructure


Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.


Data Quality & Governance


Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.


Collaboration & Stakeholders


Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.


About You


You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.


Qualifications


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).
Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.


Nice to Have


Experience with data observability tooling.
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.


Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.


Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.


The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.

About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company; however the people in Spirii have extensive experience working with electric cars and charging, as well as the development of future transport and energy systems.

Spirii is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Spirii strives to create an inclusive and diverse workplace, and we encourage all qualified applicants to apply regardless of age, gender, race, sexual/religious orientation, or disability.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Veo is a global leader in AI-based sports camera technology. Our innovative, fully automatic camera solution enables sports teams to record matches and training sessions without a camera operator. We’re democratizing the world of sports by granting video analysis for teams on all levels—a privilege that used to be only for the few. More than 50,000 clubs in 90+ countries record their games every week.

Growing as fast as we do in Veo means that every day is different, exciting, and challenging, both on the front line and in the back office.

But that’s not the most remarkable thing about us.

The coolest thing is our people. We’ve attracted some of the brightest minds in the industry. They are the reason we can create a great product and do it while enjoying ourselves.

As a Machine Learning Engineer at Veo you will get to work on challenging problems and directly contribute to the value created by our AI-driven end-user products.

You will become part of our AI team, which comprises 15 researchers and engineers responsible for all stages in the machine learning lifecycles across different projects - from scoping and defining data annotation tasks to modeling, validation, and deployment. You will be free to determine the directions of the project you work on while also getting feedback and being encouraged to spar with the rest of the team to assist each other in improving and succeeding.

We stay current with the latest research and continuously discuss concepts and ideas in recent papers to assess their relevance to our tasks and product-specific challenges.

We are looking to add yet another ambitious, high-performing junior to senior engineer/researcher with proven experience from real-world machine learning projects to the team. Experience with computer vision is advantageous but not a must.

What you will do:



Train state-of-the-art machine learning models
Write efficient inference pipelines for cloud and edge
Define and supervise data annotation tasks
Improve evaluation schemes to increase understanding of model performance and shortcomings
Contribute to long-term planning and prioritization of tasks




What you will need:



MSc or PhD in a relevant field
Hands-on experience with real-world, large-scale machine-learning projects
Great coding skills
Skilled at both autonomous tasks and contributing to group efforts
Ability to examine details closely and critically




Our Copenhagen office mirrors our innovative technology – with an indoor ball court, rooftop terrace, and well-equipped gym. We have created a facility that supports connecting disciplines across the business, facilitates creative thinking, and gives the space to engage with colleagues, our global partners, and the entire Veo community.

Values we hold at Veo:

Dare - We take initiative, stay curious, and challenge the status quo in our work.

Own it - We drive impact by identifying opportunities, taking responsibility, and confidently executing.

Level up - We embrace growth, continuously learning and shaping our skills.

Play as a team - We collaborate across a diverse organization, leveraging shared insights for stronger

outcomes.

Customer Centric - We deliver value by having close partnerships and a deep understanding of our customers (internally and externally)

We value diversity and inclusion and welcome applicants from all backgrounds, even if your experience doesn’t completely match every qualification listed. What matters most is your creativity, potential, and ability to make meaningful contributions.

Title: AI Trainer - Freelance Data Annotator
Description:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.

At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence.

What We Do

The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.

About The Role

Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses - when projects are available.

Responsibilities:


Carefully review provided data (text, images, or videos)
Label or classify content based on project guidelines
Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear material


Important note: This is project-based work. Tasks are available only when projects are active. You may be invited to one or more projects depending on your profile and current opportunities.

Each project has its own compensation level based on scope and expertise required. On this project, AI trainers earn up to $22 per hour equivalent.

Requirements


Bachelor's degree in any discipline
Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role
Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities
Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions
Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed
Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence


Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?


Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments
Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio
Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise

Title: Software Developer
Place: Furesø Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join Us in Building the Future of Financial Advisory

Festina Finance develops modern, high‑performance software used by banks and financial institutions across the Nordics and Europe. Our Advisor platform is a 360° financial advisory solution that helps bank and pension advisors guide customers through credit, mortgage, pension, wealth, and investment decisions.

Advisor is designed to be country‑agnostic and is already live in Denmark, with active expansion into the Nordics, EU, and the UK. We are now looking for a skilled Software Developer to help shape the next generation of the platform.




Why This Role Matters

Financial advisors rely on Advisor to deliver precise, compliant, and trustworthy guidance to their customers. The platform must handle complex financial logic, integrate seamlessly with banking systems, and scale across countries, products, and regulatory environments.




As a developer on the Advisor team, you will:

build the financial modelling foundation that powers real advisory conversations
ensure that forecasts, key figures, and recommendations are correct and explainable
design integrations that connect Advisor to the broader banking ecosystem
contribute to a platform used daily by advisors across multiple markets




Your work directly influences how financial decisions are made, from mortgages and pensions to investments and long‑term planning.




About the Role

You will join a cross‑functional team of developers, business analysts, and domain experts working on the core of the Advisor platform. The role combines deep domain logic with solid engineering practices, and you will work across modelling, integrations, data flows, and system behavior.

You will be involved in the full development life cycle: from understanding requirements and refining domain logic to implementing features, improving performance, and ensuring long‑term maintainability.




What You Will Work On

Develop and maintain the 360° financial modelling foundation across credit, pensions, wealth, and investments
Build and improve integrations with external banking systems and financial APIs
Work with complex data models in a layered, multi‑customer, multi‑country architecture
Collaborate with analysts, domain experts, and customers to turn requirements into production‑ready code
Ensure code quality through testing, documentation, and thorough code review




How We Build Software

Java‑based backend with strong domain‑driven design principles
Cloud‑native architecture — containerized with Docker and orchestrated on Kubernetes
Multi‑tenant, layered platform designed for country‑ and customer‑specific extensibility
REST APIs with OpenAPI specifikations
Strong emphasis on automated testing, static analysis, CI/CD, and code quality
Increasingly AI‑augmented workflows — we invest actively in AI tooling and expect developers to help shape how we use it




What You Bring

You are a developer who enjoys working with domain‑rich systems and solving real business problems through high‑quality engineering.

You have experience with, or a genuine interest in the financial domain, whether that’s banking, credit, pension, insurance, or similar areas.

We welcome applicants at different stages of their career – whether you are fresh out of university and ready to learn, or you bring some hands-on experience and want to take the next step.

You’re naturally curious and enjoy learning new domains, technologies, or better ways of solving problems. Collaboration comes easily to you, and you like working closely with developers, analysts, domain experts, and customers. You communicate openly, take initiative, and aren’t afraid to ask questions or challenge assumptions when something doesn’t add up.

Most importantly, you care about the why behind what you build — not just the how — and you take pride in delivering solutions that are robust, well‑designed, and built to last.




About Festina Finance

Festina Finance is a software company that focuses on building life policy administration systems for the pension sector and 360-degree advisory solutions for the financial sector.




40+ customers; we assist a range of banks, insurance companies, pension funds, and pension administration companies across Europe in providing the best service to their clients. The solutions include the tools necessary for calculations, optimizations, and forecasting in the most frequently used financial areas.




100+ employees and growing; Since we welcomed our first customer in 2014, we have grown from less than 10 to more than 100 employees and continue to grow. We have branches in Denmark, the Netherlands, and the UK, with HQ located in Værløse in Denmark.




Highly experienced team, where a significant part of the team has 10 – 30 years of experience in the sector with a proven track record of several successful life and Pension policy administration implementations.




You are welcome to read more about Festina Finance on our website, www.festinafinance.com

Title: Client Advisor - CELINE - Copenhagen
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
POSITION : CLIENT ADVISOR

PERMANENT CONTRACT / COPENHAGEN




CELINE, founded in 1945 by Céline Vipiana, is an iconic French Leather-Goods, Fashion & Couture House belonging to the LVMH Group. To have a chance of joining CELINE, you must thrive as a team player in a fast-paced environment amongst passionate, agile, and authentic people.




YOUR WORK ENVIRONMENT




As a Client Advisor at CELINE, you will report to the Team Manager and play a key role in driving sales and delivering an exceptional client experience. Your passion for our collections and commitment to excellence will enable you to build meaningful connections with clients, embodying the sophistication and timelessness of the Maison. Collaborating with your team, you will contribute to upholding CELINE’s prestigious brand image in every interaction.




YOUR IMPACT




Business Development

Contribute to the store’s business success by achieving and exceeding sales targets, demonstrating a strong commercial mindset, and deep product knowledge.
Proactively engage with clients to understand their needs, offering tailored recommendations and complementary products that enhance their experience with CELINE. Utilize cross-selling techniques to maximize sales opportunities and introduce clients to the full range of CELINE’s offerings.




Team and People

Foster a positive team spirit by collaborating closely with your colleagues, supporting each other in achieving both individual and collective goals.
Contribute to a harmonious work environment where every team member feels valued and motivated to deliver their best.




Client Development

Deliver an exceptional client experience by welcoming, advising, and providing unparalleled service to each client, ensuring their journey with CELINE is memorable.
Build and nurture lasting relationships with clients through personalized clienteling, cultivating loyalty and long-term connections. Leverage cross-selling strategies to deepen client engagement and drive repeat purchases.




Operations Excellence

Uphold CELINE’s standards of compliance by adhering to internal procedures and guidelines, ensuring smooth and efficient store operations.
Be actively part of the operational life of the store: Visual merchandising, restocking, transfers, after-sales service, inventory management.




Image & Brand

Serve as a CELINE ambassador, embodying the Maison’s values and excellence in every client interaction.
Share the rich history and stories behind our products, captivating clients with engaging storytelling that deepens their connection to the brand.
Maintain the highest standards of visual merchandising and personal presentation, contributing to the elegant and sophisticated image of CELINE.




KEY FOR SUCCESS




Experience:

You bring at least 1 year of professional experience in sales, within the fashion or luxury industry.




Competencies:




Passion for luxury fashion and a deep understanding of CELINE's products, heritage, and brand identity.
Client-centric mindset with a focus on delivering exceptional experiences and building lasting relationships.
A passion for challenges and a collective drive to achieve set objectives.
Initiative, a sense of service, natural curiosity, open-mindedness, optimism, and empathy are crucial for success at CELINE.
Adaptability and flexibility to meet the demands of a dynamic retail environment.
Strong ethical compass and commitment to compliance and corporate social responsibility.




Languages:

French and English, proficiency of other language(s) will be an advantage




OTHER INFORMATION




Store location: Copenhagen, Denmark




OUR COMMITMENT




As part of our #CELINECARES approach, CELINE is committed to building an inclusive and respectful work environment where every employee can develop and thrive.




We value diversity in all its forms — origins, identities, orientations, generations, abilities and life situations — and implement concrete actions to promote inclusion. Our disability policy aims to ensure equal opportunities for all our talents, with personalized accommodations if necessary.




If you require specific assistance during the recruitment process, please do not hesitate to let us know.




At CELINE, we believe that the strength of our Maison lies in the uniqueness of our employees.

Title: Senior AI Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Company Description




Tepui Agentic Labs is a Copenhagen-based EU-sovereign agentic AI data platform. We help enterprises migrate off legacy databases and ERP systems into modern AI-ready data warehouses. Our agents compress what consultancies used to deliver in 12 to 18 months into 4 to 6 weeks.

Our flagship product, Datarcon, automates the modernization of legacy enterprise data into modern analytics-ready formats, running on EU-sovereign infrastructure and fully compliant with the EU AI Act. We currently operate a production multi-agent platform with active enterprise pipeline across restaurants, auto parts, social housing, manufacturing, and energy.




Role Description

The Senior AI Engineer will work directly alongside the founder to build and scale Tepui's agentic AI platform. Responsibilities include designing new AI agents for emerging verticals, maintaining and improving the multi-agent orchestration layer, owning agent reliability and evaluation, and optimizing inference for our use cases. The Senior AI Engineer will pair with the founder on architectural decisions and grow into platform leadership as Tepui scales. This is a part-time role during pre-seed with conversion to full-time at seed close, hybrid based in Copenhagen.




Qualifications

5+ years building production Python systems with strong software engineering fundamentals
Deep hands-on experience with LLMs in production: agent design, prompt engineering, reliability, evaluation
Production experience with agent orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, LangChain, or equivalent)
Experience running and tuning open-source LLMs in production
Experience with local model deployment and GPU hardware is a strong plus
Strong Python backend framework experience (FastAPI or equivalent)
Rust experience is a strong advantage
Comfortable with relational databases, Docker, and EU-sovereign cloud infrastructure
Business and product mindset: ability to translate enterprise needs into technical solutions and contribute to product direction
Danish fluency or Scandinavian background is a strong advantage
Prior startup or founding team experience is an advantage
MSc in Computer Science, AI, or related field from ITU, DTU, KU, or equivalent




Compensation

Pre-seed stage. Equity-based compensation with conversion to full Danish market salary at seed close (planned H2 2026).




To Apply

Send your LinkedIn profile or CV plus a short paragraph about the most interesting agent or LLM system you've built. info@tepuilabs.dk

Title: AI Scientist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At H1, we believe access to the best healthcare information is a basic human right. Our mission is to provide a platform that can optimally inform every doctor interaction globally. This promotes health equity and builds needed trust in healthcare systems. To accomplish this our teams harness the power of data and AI-technology to unlock groundbreaking medical insights and convert those insights into action that result in optimal patient outcomes and accelerates an equitable and inclusive drug development lifecycle. Visit h1.co to learn more about us.

We are looking for an AI Scientist to join a dedicated team building and evolving a clinical data platform serving the clinical operations space. You will design and implement machine learning models that power insights across the clinical trial lifecycle — working with large-scale healthcare datasets spanning publications, clinical trials, medical claims, and provider data.

This is a backend, data-intensive role. You will work at the intersection of machine learning research and production data systems, building models that run at scale and integrate into real-world clinical workflows. You should be comfortable moving from research to implementation in a fast-paced, applied setting.

What You'll Do At H1


Design, develop, and deploy machine learning models to extract insights from clinical and healthcare data — including NLP tasks across publications, trial records, and medical claims
Build and optimize ML pipelines on large-scale distributed systems using Apache Spark and cloud-native tools
Work with structured and unstructured data sources to develop predictive models, classification systems, and entity resolution approaches
Collaborate with data engineers to integrate ML models into production ETL pipelines and ensure scalability and reliability
Partner with MLOps teams to operationalize models — including monitoring, retraining, and versioning workflows
Evaluate and benchmark model performance and present findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Stay current with advances in NLP, deep learning, and healthcare-specific AI applications to identify opportunities for innovation


About You


A rigorous, applied scientist who moves confidently from research to production — you build models that ship
Someone who understands the realities of working with messy, large-scale healthcare data and can design around its limitations
Collaborative and communicative — comfortable working with data engineers, MLOps practitioners, and product teams
Curious about the clinical space and quick to develop domain intuition around trial processes, provider data, and healthcare terminology
Confident owning technical decisions and presenting trade-offs clearly to both technical and business stakeholders


Requirements


5+ years of hands-on experience in machine learning, data science, or applied AI roles
Strong proficiency in Python; working knowledge of SQL and R
Deep experience with ML frameworks such as TensorFlow and PyTorch
Proven experience building and running models on large-scale data pipelines using Apache Spark (PySpark)
Exposure to NLP techniques — including text classification, named entity recognition, information extraction, and transformer-based models
Familiarity with cloud platforms (Azure preferred) and tools such as Databricks, Delta Lake, and Kubernetes
Experience working closely with data engineering and MLOps teams in production environments
Background in healthcare, life sciences, pharma, or clinical research is highly preferred
Comfortable working independently in a remote setting with a distributed, cross-timezone team


H1 OFFERS


Generous paid time off & pre-planned company-wide wellness holidays
Retirement options
Health & charitable donation stipends
Impactful Business Resource Groups
Flexible work hours & the opportunity to work from anywhere
The opportunity to work with leading biotech and life sciences companies in an innovative industry with a mission to improve healthcare around the globe


H1 is proud to be an equal opportunity employer that celebrates diversity and is committed to creating an inclusive workplace with equal opportunity for all applicants and teammates. Our goal is to recruit the most talented people from a diverse candidate pool regardless of race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex (including pregnancy), age, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

H1 is committed to working with and providing access and reasonable accommodation to applicants with mental and/or physical disabilities. If you require an accommodation, please reach out to your recruiter once you've begun the interview process. All requests for accommodations are treated discreetly and confidentially, as practical and permitted by law.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Senior Data Scientist




Vil du arbejde med ML-, GenAI- og agentbaserede løsninger, der rent faktisk kommer i drift – og hvor tekniske valg har direkte konsekvenser for forretning og beslutninger? Er du interesseret i, hvordan AI kan bruges til at skabe konkret værdi i den offentlige sektor og i andre regulerede brancher som finans og farma?

Vi søger en senior data scientist, der både kan modellere på højt niveau og engagere sig substantielt i de tekniske og arkitektoniske valg, der afgør om en løsning kommer i produktion. Hvis du trives med at tage ansvar, arbejde tæt med kunder og være med til at præge et stærkt fagligt miljø i et hus i hastig vækst, er du måske den rette tilføjelse til vores team.




Din rolle

Du bliver en del af vores Data & AI-team og arbejder i projekter, hvor data, modeller og systemer hænger tæt sammen. Rollen indebærer både udvikling og implementering og en tæt dialog om, hvordan løsningerne overdrages og anvendes i praksis. Du vil i mange projekter fungere som teknisk drivkraft og være ansvarlig for centrale designvalg og kvaliteten af de løsninger, vi leverer.

Eksempler på projekter:

·      RAG-løsning til understøttelse af sagsbehandling i offentlig sektor

·      Forecasting-model til finansielle beslutningsprocesser

·      GenAI-assistent til automatisering af interne arbejdsprocesser

·      Agentbaseret arkitektur til migrering af legacy-system

I praksis vil din hverdag bestå af opgaver som at:

·      Udvikle og implementere ML- og GenAI-løsninger (klassifikation, regression, forecasting, NLP, RAG, agentflows)

·      Tage modeller fra prototype til stabil drift

·      Bidrage til tekniske og arkitektoniske beslutninger

·      Tæt dialog med både kunder og kollegaer om at omsætte behov til løsninger

·      Bidrage med dataforståelse, feature engineering og klargøring til produktion (test, versionering, integration)

·      Udvikle nye typer ydelser og præsentere oplæg og tilbud

Din hverdag vil typisk bestå af en kombination af hands-on udvikling, dialog med kunder og sparring med kollegaer om design og implementering. Du forventes også at kunne indgå i det markedsvendte arbejde med at udvikle nye kommercielle ydelser og lave/præsentere oplæg og tilbud for kunder.




Kvalifikationer

Minimum:

·      Solid erfaring med Python og relevante ML-biblioteker

·      Erfaring med at udvikle og implementere ML-modeller i praksis

·      Erfaring med at bringe modeller fra analyse til produktion

·      Erfaring med data pipelines, model performance og trade-offs

·      Flydende dansk (skrift og tale)

Højt vægtet:

·      Erfaring med produktionssætning af ML-/GenAI-løsninger i komplekse systemlandskaber

·      Erfaring med cloud (fx Azure, Databricks) og deployment patterns

·      Erfaring med GenAI (fx RAG, LLM-baserede løsninger eller agentflows)

·      Erfaring med at tage teknisk ansvar og træffe arkitekturbeslutninger

·      Evne til at arbejde tæt med forretning og omsætte behov til løsninger




Det er et plus, hvis du har:

·      Erfaring fra konsulentbranchen

·      Kendskab til den offentlige sektor eller andre regulerede brancher

·      Interesse for AI-strategi og use case-udvikling

·      God forståelse for it-arkitektur




Personlige kompetencer:

·      Analytisk og struktureret

·      Kan forklare komplekse problemstillinger klart

·      Tager ansvar og arbejder selvstændigt

·      Samarbejdsorienteret og nysgerrig

·      Dyb interesse for teknologi og AI




Det får du hos os

Hos Ajeto arbejder vi med nogle af de mest udfordrende data- og systemudviklingsprojekter. Du vil være en del af et hands-on-orienteret og stimulerende miljø, hvor du både kan videreudvikle din egen faglighed og være med til at præge virksomhedens faglige profil. Du bliver en del af et team med stærke kompetencer inden for både data, engineering og strategi.




Du vil:

·      Arbejde med reelle produktionsløsninger

·      Få tidligt ansvar og mulighed for at påvirke løsninger og retning

·      Være i tæt sparring med erfarne profiler, både tekniske og forretningsmæssige

·      Have struktureret faglig udvikling gennem bl.a. mentorordning

·      Deltage i konferencer og faglige miljøer

·      Opnå konkurrencedygtig løn og vilkår

·      Være en del af et stærkt socialt fællesskab med samvær, mange fælles aktiviteter, årlig strategitur mv.

Projekter foregår primært i København med begrænset rejseaktivitet. Arbejdsformen varierer mellem on-site og remote afhængigt af projekt og kunde. Vi vægter fleksibilitet højt.

 

Om Ajeto

Ajeto blev etableret i 2024 af erfarne profiler fra it- og konsulentbranchen med ambitionen om at bygge et stærkt alternativ på det danske konsulentmarked med særlig styrke i den offentlige sektor og i regulerede brancher såsom finans og farma, hvor strategi, data og systemudvikling skal tænkes og leveres samlet, og hvor vi tager ansvar for hele kæden fra retning og behovsafklaring til implementering.

Vi arbejder i organisationer med komplekse beslutningsprocesser og mange interessenter. Det kræver, at vi kombinerer analytisk styrke og teknisk gennemførelse med forståelse for de institutionelle rammer og beslutningsrum, organisationer opererer i.

Vi er et konsulenthus i vækst. Dette understøttes af vores hidtidige vækst, hvor vi på under to år gået til at være snart 40 konsulenter. For dig betyder det, at du bliver en del af en udvikling, hvor vi fejrer succeserne sammen, mens du og vi løbende tilpasser os til en voksende organisation. Vores ambition er endvidere at tage en tydelig position inden for modernisering, AI og datadrevne løsninger i Danmark. For dig betyder det mulighed for stort ansvar og indflydelse.




Løn og vilkår

Du ansættes som konsulent og i den lønramme på dette niveau. Hos Ajeto ønsker vi ikke at incentivisere gennem en uigennemsigtig bonus-model, som du selv kun har begrænset indflydelse på. Lønmodellen består derfor af en fast, konkurrencedygtig løn, som giver dig pengene i hånden fra start.

Vi ønsker at skabe de rammer, som gør det muligt for dig at yde dit bedste arbejde på en måde hvor du trives, både når du er på arbejde, og når du er hjemme. Ajeto tilbyder derfor også fem feriefridage i tillæg til ferielovens fem ugers ferie, samt mulighed for fleksibelt arbejde i det omfang, som det kan lade sig gøre på dit aktuelle kundeprojekt.




Hvordan søger du

Send os din ansøgning senest den 12. juni 2026 på: job@ajeto.dk

Introducer dig selv til os som var du allerede vores kollega, og giv os indsigt i dine faglige interesser, styrker og ambitioner. Fortæl os også gerne om dine tidligere erfaringer, roller eller projekter, som du er særlig stolt over. Fortæl os hvorfor du er interesseret i dette job, og hvad du håber på at opnå i din tid hos Ajeto, fx inden for de kommende 1-3 år.




Hvad sker der efter du har søgt jobbet?

Vi vil løbende indkalde relevante kandidater til interview. Du kan forvente et svar på din ansøgning senest den 18. juni 2026.




Hvis du bliver inviteret til samtale, kan der forekomme en case. I så fald gennemgår vi den sammen, hvorefter du får forberedelsestid og derefter blive bedt om at præsentere din tilgang. Casen vil danne rammen om den faglige dialog på ansættelsessamtalen og har alene til formål at give ansøgeren muligheden for at samle sine tanker om scenariet, og italesætte hvordan kandidaten går til opgaver.




Vi opfordrer ansøgere med enhver baggrund at søge dette job; et job hvor vi sammen vil arbejde for at du kan udføre din karrieres bedste arbejde. Vi sætter pris på, at du overvejer at søge hos os, og vi lover at give dig og din ansøgning vores fulde opmærksomhed. Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig!




Forventet opstart

Hvis du bliver tilbudt stillingen, er der forventet opstart den 1. september 2026. Skulle der opstå en mulighed for at starte tidligere eller senere, som både matcher dine ønsker og vores kunders behov, er vi åbne for en fleksibel opstartsdato.




Spørgsmål

For spørgsmål til stillingen bedes du kontakte partner Thor Hvidbak (thv@ajeto.dk, 40447405).

Er du interesseret i, hvordan AI kan bruges til at skabe konkret værdi i den offentlige sektor og i andre regulerede brancher som finans og farma?

Vi søger en senior data scientist, der både kan modellere på højt niveau og engagere sig substantielt i de tekniske og arkitektoniske valg, der afgør om en løsning kommer i produktion. Hvis du trives med at tage ansvar, arbejde tæt med kunder og være med til at præge et stærkt fagligt miljø i et hus i hastig vækst, er du måske den rette tilføjelse til vores team.


Din rolle

Du bliver en del af vores Data & AI-team og arbejder i projekter, hvor data, modeller og systemer hænger tæt sammen. Rollen indebærer både udvikling og implementering og en tæt dialog om, hvordan løsningerne overdrages og anvendes i praksis. Du vil i mange projekter fungere som teknisk drivkraft og være ansvarlig for centrale designvalg og kvaliteten af de løsninger, vi leverer.

Eksempler på projekter:

·      RAG-løsning til understøttelse af sagsbehandling i offentlig sektor

·      Forecasting-model til finansielle beslutningsprocesser

·      GenAI-assistent til automatisering af interne arbejdsprocesser

·      Agentbaseret arkitektur til migrering af legacy-system

I praksis vil din hverdag bestå af opgaver som at:

·      Udvikle og implementere ML- og GenAI-løsninger (klassifikation, regression, forecasting, NLP, RAG, agentflows)

·      Tage modeller fra prototype til stabil drift

·      Bidrage til tekniske og arkitektoniske beslutninger

·      Tæt dialog med både kunder og kollegaer om at omsætte behov til løsninger

·      Bidrage med dataforståelse, feature engineering og klargøring til produktion (test, versionering, integration)

·      Udvikle nye typer ydelser og præsentere oplæg og tilbud

Din hverdag vil typisk bestå af en kombination af hands-on udvikling, dialog med kunder og sparring med kollegaer om design og implementering. Du forventes også at kunne indgå i det markedsvendte arbejde med at udvikle nye kommercielle ydelser og lave/præsentere oplæg og tilbud for kunder.


Kvalifikationer

Minimum:

·      Solid erfaring med Python og relevante ML-biblioteker

·      Erfaring med at udvikle og implementere ML-modeller i praksis

·      Erfaring med at bringe modeller fra analyse til produktion

·      Erfaring med data pipelines, model performance og trade-offs

·      Flydende dansk (skrift og tale)

Højt vægtet:

·      Erfaring med produktionssætning af ML-/GenAI-løsninger i komplekse systemlandskaber

·      Erfaring med cloud (fx Azure, Databricks) og deployment patterns

·      Erfaring med GenAI (fx RAG, LLM-baserede løsninger eller agentflows)

·      Erfaring med at tage teknisk ansvar og træffe arkitekturbeslutninger

·      Evne til at arbejde tæt med forretning og omsætte behov til løsninger


Det er et plus, hvis du har:

·      Erfaring fra konsulentbranchen

·      Kendskab til den offentlige sektor eller andre regulerede brancher

·      Interesse for AI-strategi og use case-udvikling

·      God forståelse for it-arkitektur


Personlige kompetencer:

·      Analytisk og struktureret

·      Kan forklare komplekse problemstillinger klart

·      Tager ansvar og arbejder selvstændigt

·      Samarbejdsorienteret og nysgerrig

·      Dyb interesse for teknologi og AI


Det får du hos os

Hos Ajeto arbejder vi med nogle af de mest udfordrende data- og systemudviklingsprojekter. Du vil være en del af et hands-on-orienteret og stimulerende miljø, hvor du både kan videreudvikle din egen faglighed og være med til at præge virksomhedens faglige profil. Du bliver en del af et team med stærke kompetencer inden for både data, engineering og strategi.


Du vil:

·      Arbejde med reelle produktionsløsninger

·      Få tidligt ansvar og mulighed for at påvirke løsninger og retning

·      Være i tæt sparring med erfarne profiler, både tekniske og forretningsmæssige

·      Have struktureret faglig udvikling gennem bl.a. mentorordning

·      Deltage i konferencer og faglige miljøer

·      Opnå konkurrencedygtig løn og vilkår

·      Være en del af et stærkt socialt fællesskab med samvær, mange fælles aktiviteter, årlig strategitur mv.

Projekter foregår primært i København med begrænset rejseaktivitet. Arbejdsformen varierer mellem on-site og remote afhængigt af projekt og kunde. Vi vægter fleksibilitet højt.

 

Om Ajeto

Ajeto blev etableret i 2024 af erfarne profiler fra it- og konsulentbranchen med ambitionen om at bygge et stærkt alternativ på det danske konsulentmarked med særlig styrke i den offentlige sektor og i regulerede brancher såsom finans og farma, hvor strategi, data og systemudvikling skal tænkes og leveres samlet, og hvor vi tager ansvar for hele kæden fra retning og behovsafklaring til implementering.

Vi arbejder i organisationer med komplekse beslutningsprocesser og mange interessenter. Det kræver, at vi kombinerer analytisk styrke og teknisk gennemførelse med forståelse for de institutionelle rammer og beslutningsrum, organisationer opererer i.

Vi er et konsulenthus i vækst. Dette understøttes af vores hidtidige vækst, hvor vi på under to år gået til at være snart 40 konsulenter. For dig betyder det, at du bliver en del af en udvikling, hvor vi fejrer succeserne sammen, mens du og vi løbende tilpasser os til en voksende organisation. Vores ambition er endvidere at tage en tydelig position inden for modernisering, AI og datadrevne løsninger i Danmark. For dig betyder det mulighed for stort ansvar og indflydelse.


Løn og vilkår

Du ansættes som konsulent og i den lønramme på dette niveau. Hos Ajeto ønsker vi ikke at incentivisere gennem en uigennemsigtig bonus-model, som du selv kun har begrænset indflydelse på. Lønmodellen består derfor af en fast, konkurrencedygtig løn, som giver dig pengene i hånden fra start.

Vi ønsker at skabe de rammer, som gør det muligt for dig at yde dit bedste arbejde på en måde hvor du trives, både når du er på arbejde, og når du er hjemme. Ajeto tilbyder derfor også fem feriefridage i tillæg til ferielovens fem ugers ferie, samt mulighed for fleksibelt arbejde i det omfang, som det kan lade sig gøre på dit aktuelle kundeprojekt.


Hvordan søger du

Send os din ansøgning senest den 12. juni 2026 på: job@ajeto.dk

Introducer dig selv til os som var du allerede vores kollega, og giv os indsigt i dine faglige interesser, styrker og ambitioner. Fortæl os også gerne om dine tidligere erfaringer, roller eller projekter, som du er særlig stolt over. Fortæl os hvorfor du er interesseret i dette job, og hvad du håber på at opnå i din tid hos Ajeto, fx inden for de kommende 1-3 år.


Hvad sker der efter du har søgt jobbet?

Vi vil løbende indkalde relevante kandidater til interview. Du kan forvente et svar på din ansøgning senest den 18. juni 2026.


Hvis du bliver inviteret til samtale, kan der forekomme en case. I så fald gennemgår vi den sammen, hvorefter du får forberedelsestid og derefter blive bedt om at præsentere din tilgang. Casen vil danne rammen om den faglige dialog på ansættelsessamtalen og har alene til formål at give ansøgeren muligheden for at samle sine tanker om scenariet, og italesætte hvordan kandidaten går til opgaver.


Vi opfordrer ansøgere med enhver baggrund at søge dette job; et job hvor vi sammen vil arbejde for at du kan udføre din karrieres bedste arbejde. Vi sætter pris på, at du overvejer at søge hos os, og vi lover at give dig og din ansøgning vores fulde opmærksomhed. Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig!


Forventet opstart

Hvis du bliver tilbudt stillingen, er der forventet opstart den 1. september 2026. Skulle der opstå en mulighed for at starte tidligere eller senere, som både matcher dine ønsker og vores kunders behov, er vi åbne for en fleksibel opstartsdato.


Spørgsmål

For spørgsmål til stillingen bedes du kontakte partner Thor Hvidbak (thv@ajeto.dk, 40447405).

Title: AI Strategy & Enablement Lead
Description:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI Strategy & Enablement Lead in Denmark.

This role sits at the core of enterprise-wide AI transformation, shaping how artificial intelligence is embedded into day-to-day operations, decision-making, and productivity across teams. You will act as a strategic connector between leadership and execution, identifying high-impact opportunities where AI can meaningfully improve workflows and business outcomes. Working closely with functional leaders, you will define priorities, drive adoption, and ensure accountability for measurable results. The environment is fast-moving, highly cross-functional, and deeply focused on practical AI application rather than experimentation for its own sake. You will also build internal capability by enabling teams to design and scale their own AI workflows. This is a high-impact role where strategic thinking, execution rigor, and change leadership converge.

Accountabilities


Lead AI enablement initiatives across the organization by identifying and scaling high-value use cases across functions
Partner with senior stakeholders to define AI priorities, align on strategy, and drive adoption of AI-powered ways of working
Act as the key connector between leadership and execution, translating business needs into actionable AI initiatives
Build scalable AI literacy programs, including training, onboarding materials, prompt libraries, and reusable workflows
Drive adoption and optimization of existing AI tools, ensuring maximum value from current technology investments
Own enterprise AI knowledge-sharing systems and champion best practices across teams
Track AI industry developments and recommend what to adopt, pilot, or deprioritize based on business relevance
Measure and report on AI impact through key metrics such as productivity gains, time savings, and business ROI
Ensure responsible AI usage in collaboration with security, compliance, and leadership stakeholders
Support cross-functional execution of AI initiatives and ensure sustained adoption through structured change management


Requirements


3-6 years of experience in AI enablement, transformation, strategy, operations, or high-growth tech environments
Strong practical fluency with AI tools, with the ability to apply and scale them across business functions
Proven experience leading change initiatives and driving organizational adoption of new tools or ways of working
Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence from individual contributors to executive leadership
Strong coaching mindset with experience building capability and enabling teams to work independently
Highly structured and analytical thinking, with the ability to translate ambiguity into clear plans and outcomes
Strong communication and storytelling skills to align stakeholders and drive shared understanding
Bias for action, experimentation, and continuous improvement in fast-changing environments
Nice to have: experience with prompt engineering, BI tools (e.g., Looker or similar), or AI-assisted analytics workflows
Nice to have: exposure to fintech, SaaS, or payments environments, and program management experience


Benefits


Fully remote and globally distributed work culture with strong flexibility
Competitive compensation package with share options
Uncapped holiday allowance with a minimum of 25 days off
Private medical insurance coverage
Learning and development budget to support continuous growth
Access to co-working spaces and global workation opportunities
Company retreats and in-person collaboration sessions
Home office setup support (up to £500)
High-quality equipment provided for your role
Broad set of additional perks depending on location


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Scientific Data Engineer - Preclinical Neuroscience
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Scientific Data Engineer – Preclinical Neuroscience

Do you enjoy building scalable data solutions and workflows? Does applying your data engineering skills across bioinformatics and in vivo neuroscience data – from gene expression and omics data to electrophysiology and behavior – sound like an exciting prospect? Then you could be our new Scientific Data Engineer to join our preclinical Neuroscience area!

Your new role

This is a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of neuroscience, data engineering, and next-generation drug discovery for people living with brain diseases. Working across in vitro, ex vivo and in vivo domains, you will play a key role in shaping how diverse preclinical data is captured, structured, analyzed, and made accessible across the organization. Your work will directly enable scientists to generate insights that advance both early- and late-stage drug discovery projects in our portfolio.

Your future team

You will reside in the Department of Single Cell Pharmacology & Bioinformatics within the Neuroscience organization, where you will join a team of experienced bioinformaticians, wet-lab scientists, technicians and students. In this role, you will also work closely with the in vivo neuroscience area, specifically the Department of Data Acquisition Systems and Analytics, thereby contributing to data engineering solutions across both in vitro and in vivo domains. Across the Neuroscience area, we share a strong collaborative spirit, mutual respect, and a focus on scientific discovery, while also having engaging, varied, and fun workdays.

Together with the rest of the Neuroscience organization, we are responsible for the biological research at Lundbeck and are focused on generating pre-clinical data and insight that are necessary for advancing both early- and late-stage drug discovery projects.

The position is based in Copenhagen, Denmark. Fully remote applications will not be considered

What You Bring To The Team


You have a master’s or PhD degree in Bioinformatics, Computer Science or similar, and are comfortable with relevant bioinformatics and in vivo neuroscience data types that you wield with your comprehensive and effective coding skills (Python and R are the standard)
Preferably, you have a solid grasp of fundamental biological concepts and/or drug discovery which allow you to contribute with innovative solutions to biological questions for our preclinical projects
You have hands-on experience building and operating robust data pipelines in the cloud (AWS), including infrastructure as code (AWS CDK)
Your experience includes working with complex scientific data sets such as omics, time series data, and large files (e.g., video or imaging), and you enjoy building reliable systems and workflows that enable others
You are familiar with workflow orchestration tools (e.g., Nextflow, Dagster) and containerized workloads (e.g., Docker) and have experience with modern data stack technologies such as Snowflake and dbt
You are a collaborative and delivery-focused team player who is comfortable working across multiple teams and functions



Our promise to you

Lundbeck offers an inspiring workplace and innovative culture, where our curiosity, accountability and adaptability enable us to transform lives. We want to go faster and further on addressing the big unmet needs of people living with brain disorders. We offer rewarding careers with a mix of exciting tasks and development opportunities that are balanced with initiatives focused on your well-being.

We need every brain in the game, and at Lundbeck, we are committed to building a workforce that is as diverse as the people we serve. Read more about our commitment at www.lundbeck.com/global/about-us/our-commitment/diversity-and-inclusion.

Annual Base Salary Range: 582 000 - 799 700 DKK gross

At Lundbeck, we are committed to fair and transparent pay. Please note that the final base salary within the stated range will be based on relevant qualifications, skills, competencies, and level of proficiency as well as internal pay equity.

Apply now

Can you see yourself in this role? We want to hear from you. Does this sound like your dream job, but you’re not sure if you meet all the requirements? We still want to hear from you!

Upload your CV and include a few lines about your motivation for applying. We ask you not to include a photo or personal information that does not relate to your professional experience.

Applications must be received by June 10, 2026.

Learn more about us at www.lundbeck.com, LinkedIn or Instagram (@h_lundbeck).

#EveryBrainInTheGame

This job ad is intended for individuals seeking a career opportunity with Lundbeck. We engage with recruitment and search firms where needed on the basis of a written agreement, and we do not accept unsolicited requests of any kind. If you work as a recruitment consultant, you are kindly instructed to refrain from contacting Lundbeck or the hiring manager directly with suggested candidates. If you violate this policy, you do so at your own risk and for your own account, and Lundbeck will not assume any liability nor pay any associated fees resulting from such violation.

Title: Growth Lead
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Two platforms. 500,000+ users. The traffic is there. Help us convert!

Pinploy runs Handyhand (Denmark's largest marketplace for household help) and HappyHelper (recurring household services). We went from 24M to 139M DKK in yearly volume in two years. Together we're targeting 500M DKK in yearly volume by 2028.

The product works. The demand is real. Now we need someone who obsesses over the space between "user lands" and "user converts" — and makes that gap smaller, faster.

You'll own conversion and activation across both platforms. Task creation flows, first bookings, first matches, landing pages, signup funnels every point where a visitor decides to act or bounce. You'll design experiments, pull your own data, run tests, and ship what works. You report directly to the CEO. No layers, no approval chains.

The setup

Location: Copenhagen office.

Team: You'll work directly with the CEO, three developers, a UX researcher, and a marketer. ~10 people total.

Your lane: You sit between marketing and product. You collaborate with both, but conversion experimentation is yours.

Stage: Profitable, growing fast, self-funded. 500,000+ users in Denmark, recently launched in the Netherlands.

Stack context: You won't write production code, but you'll work closely with people who do in Angular, Node.js, and React Native. You'll live in PostHog, SQL, and whatever experimentation tools get the job done.

What You'll Own


Activation & conversion across both platforms — find where users drop off in task creation, booking, and matching flows. Build hypotheses, run experiments, ship improvements.
A/B testing and experimentation — you design rigorous tests, interpret results correctly, and know when to kill, scale, or iterate. You maintain a rolling experimentation roadmap - you decide what to test next.
Data and analytics — you write SQL, work in PostHog and GA4, and pull your own answers. You don't wait for someone to build you a dashboard.
Landing page and funnel optimization — you own how entry points perform for both organic and paid traffic. You can build or edit pages without needing a developer every time.
Light paid optimization — you don't own acquisition, but you make sure paid traffic actually converts. Retargeting, landing page performance for ad campaigns, conversion tracking.
Product input from the conversion layer - your experiments and data shape what we build. You sit close enough to product and engineering to spec a test that ships in a day.
AI-assisted workflows we use LLMs heavily across the team. You'll use AI tools to move faster as a default, not a novelty.


How This Role Fits

You won't write production code — but you'll work tag managers, build landing pages, write SQL, and configure experimentation tools on your own. You'll collaborate closely with developers when tests need to go deeper.

Someone else owns acquisition and brand. Someone else owns UX research. Your lane starts where traffic arrives and ends where users convert - and you own everything in between. You find the lever and pull it.

We value people who fix things before being asked. People who try the tool before debating whether it's worth trying. People who flag problems with a proposed solution attached. People who care more about the outcome than about who was right. This probably isn't for you if you prefer well-defined processes before you start.

What We're Looking For


You've run conversion experiments at a marketplace, SaaS, or consumer platform — and moved real metrics, not just run tests.
Strong A/B testing methodology — statistical significance, sample sizes, when to kill a test, when to let it run.
Fluent in PostHog, Amplitude, Mixpanel, or similar product analytics. GA4 and tag managers are second nature.
You write SQL to answer your own questions. You don't file a ticket and wait.
You can spec a test clearly enough that a developer ships it in a day — or you handle it yourself when no dev is needed.
Analytical instinct — you reach for data before you reach for your opinion.
AI-fluent — you use LLMs as part of how you work, not as something you're evaluating.
Fluent in English. Danish is a plus but not required.


Nice to have


Experience with marketplace dynamics (two-sided supply/demand).
Paid acquisition or lifecycle marketing experience alongside CRO.
You've worked across multiple products or platforms simultaneously.
Experience helping a product enter a new market (we recently launched in the Netherlands).

HTML:
Two platforms. 500,000+ users. The traffic is there. Help us convert!

Pinploy runs Handyhand (Denmark's largest marketplace for household help) and HappyHelper (recurring household services). We went from 24M to 139M DKK in yearly volume in two years. Together we're targeting 500M DKK in yearly volume by 2028.

The product works. The demand is real. Now we need someone who obsesses over the space between "user lands" and "user converts" — and makes that gap smaller, faster.

You'll own conversion and activation across both platforms. Task creation flows, first bookings, first matches, landing pages, signup funnels every point where a visitor decides to act or bounce. You'll design experiments, pull your own data, run tests, and ship what works. You report directly to the CEO. No layers, no approval chains.

The setup

Location: Copenhagen office.

Team: You'll work directly with the CEO, three developers, a UX researcher, and a marketer. ~10 people total.

Your lane: You sit between marketing and product. You collaborate with both, but conversion experimentation is yours.

Stage: Profitable, growing fast, self-funded. 500,000+ users in Denmark, recently launched in the Netherlands.

Stack context: You won't write production code, but you'll work closely with people who do in Angular, Node.js, and React Native. You'll live in PostHog, SQL, and whatever experimentation tools get the job done.

What You'll Own

  • Activation & conversion across both platforms — find where users drop off in task creation, booking, and matching flows. Build hypotheses, run experiments, ship improvements.
  • A/B testing and experimentation — you design rigorous tests, interpret results correctly, and know when to kill, scale, or iterate. You maintain a rolling experimentation roadmap - you decide what to test next.
  • Data and analytics — you write SQL, work in PostHog and GA4, and pull your own answers. You don't wait for someone to build you a dashboard.
  • Landing page and funnel optimization — you own how entry points perform for both organic and paid traffic. You can build or edit pages without needing a developer every time.
  • Light paid optimization — you don't own acquisition, but you make sure paid traffic actually converts. Retargeting, landing page performance for ad campaigns, conversion tracking.
  • Product input from the conversion layer - your experiments and data shape what we build. You sit close enough to product and engineering to spec a test that ships in a day.
  • AI-assisted workflows we use LLMs heavily across the team. You'll use AI tools to move faster as a default, not a novelty.

How This Role Fits

You won't write production code — but you'll work tag managers, build landing pages, write SQL, and configure experimentation tools on your own. You'll collaborate closely with developers when tests need to go deeper.

Someone else owns acquisition and brand. Someone else owns UX research. Your lane starts where traffic arrives and ends where users convert - and you own everything in between. You find the lever and pull it.

We value people who fix things before being asked. People who try the tool before debating whether it's worth trying. People who flag problems with a proposed solution attached. People who care more about the outcome than about who was right. This probably isn't for you if you prefer well-defined processes before you start.

What We're Looking For

  • You've run conversion experiments at a marketplace, SaaS, or consumer platform — and moved real metrics, not just run tests.
  • Strong A/B testing methodology — statistical significance, sample sizes, when to kill a test, when to let it run.
  • Fluent in PostHog, Amplitude, Mixpanel, or similar product analytics. GA4 and tag managers are second nature.
  • You write SQL to answer your own questions. You don't file a ticket and wait.
  • You can spec a test clearly enough that a developer ships it in a day — or you handle it yourself when no dev is needed.
  • Analytical instinct — you reach for data before you reach for your opinion.
  • AI-fluent — you use LLMs as part of how you work, not as something you're evaluating.
  • Fluent in English. Danish is a plus but not required.

Nice to have

  • Experience with marketplace dynamics (two-sided supply/demand).
  • Paid acquisition or lifecycle marketing experience alongside CRO.
  • You've worked across multiple products or platforms simultaneously.
  • Experience helping a product enter a new market (we recently launched in the Netherlands).

Title: Machine Learning /AI Engineer (Senior to Principal Level)
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We're an award-winning Google Cloud partner. We're the experts in transforming businesses and shaping the future of cloud technology. Join us and collaborate with top minds, build groundbreaking solutions, and make a real difference for our clients. Enjoy extensive professional development, global reach, and a supportive, Google-inspired culture. If you're passionate about the cloud, this is where you want to be.

With over 14 years of experience in Google Cloud under our belt, offices in 18 countries and more than 600 experts, we have deployed practical innovation alongside Google. We focus on infrastructure modernisation, data management, smart analytics, application modernisation, and AI & machine learning.

Curious about our projects? We got you: Check out our success stories

Job Description

Day-to-day life


Design, develop, and deploy cutting-edge machine learning models – crafting bespoke AI solutions for our Nordic clients.
Collaborate with data scientists, data engineers, and Google experts to overcome complex challenges and deliver impactful outcomes.
Champion knowledge sharing and mentorship, guiding junior colleagues in the art of machine learning and fostering a culture of continuous learning.
Stay at the forefront of Google Cloud AI/ML technologies, continuously expanding your expertise and exploring new possibilities.
Confidently present solutions to clients, bridging the gap between complex algorithms and real-world business value, while actively cultivating and expanding those customer relationships.



Want to know more? Check out our people stories on Youtube

Qualifications

We're seeking a passionate and experienced ML Engineer with a proven track record in designing, developing, and deploying machine learning models. You'll be a cloud enthusiast with a deep understanding of AI/ML principles and hands-on experience with cloud technologies. As a coding expert, you'll possess strong Python skills and a solid foundation in machine learning frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain, LangGraph).

Excellent communication is essential – you'll build strong relationships with clients, translate their needs into innovative AI solutions, and present complex topics with clarity and confidence. You're a true collaborator, thriving in team environments where knowledge sharing and mutual support are paramount.

Fluency in English and one of the Scandinavian languages (Swedish, Norwegian, or Danish) is required

Additional Information

Why Choose Devoteam G Cloud?


Own the AI revolution: Dive into the exciting world of Generative AI and ML, playing a pivotal role in shaping projects around Google's groundbreaking Gemini foundation model.
Innovative Impact: Work on the forefront of cloud technology at a globally recognized leader in Google Cloud solutions.
Direct Google Connection: Collaborate closely with Google teams, shaping the future of cloud and AI.
Unleash Your Expertise: Thrive in a fast-paced environment where sharing knowledge and success is paramount.
End-to-End Impact: Drive complete business transformations, from strategy to implementation and beyond.
Accelerate Your Career: Grow within a Google-inspired culture, gaining specialisations and certifications.



We do plenty of activities, like meet-ups, hackathons, and partnerships. Don’t lose any of them: follow us on LinkedIn

Want to know more about life at Devoteam? Check our Instagram page

Ready to join an elite team? Apply for our Machine Learning & AI Engineer position and become part of something exceptional!

Is this not the right position for you? Don’t worry, we have plenty of opportunities! Check out our Career page
