Title: Audi Service Advisor
Description:
About The Role

We have an excellent opportunity available for a motivated Service Advisor to join our team at Nottingham Audi.

As a Sytner Service Advisor, you will provide outstanding customer care and be the vital link between our customers and the workshop. You will inform and consult on vehicle servicing, and keep the customer up to date at every stage whilst answering any questions or queries they may have. You will also liaise with technicians/workshop control about vehicle statuses and ensure that vehicles will be ready on time. You will also have the ability to give the best advice on our products and services to our customers.

This is a challenging role where customer care always comes first, you'll take the time to talk to people, build a genuine rapport and identify exactly what our customers want and need.

Sytner Service Advisors work a variety of flexible patterns which can typically include weekends to ensure we provide our customers with the highest possible levels of service.

About You

Don't worry if you do not have previous experience, we want applications from individuals with a willingness to learn and progress. Training is provided through our dedicated in-house Learning & Development team and outstanding manufacturer courses run by *[Brand]* to support your career.

Ideally, you will have excellent organisational and administrative skills along with good attention to detail. A strong customer service background would be beneficial as you will be delivering high levels of customer satisfaction in a very busy and demanding environment.

Working knowledge of Kerridge would be advantageous, but not essential.

Why Sytner?

Sytner Group are delighted to provide an industry-leading benefits package.

We are passionate about continuous improvement and building an environment where everyone feels valued, appreciated and able to reach their full potential.


Enhanced Holiday Entitlement – 33 days inc. bank holidays
Industry-leading Maternity, Paternity and Adoption Pay
Career Development
Recognition of Long Service every 5 years
Discounted Car Schemes
High Street Discounts
Discounted Gym memberships
Cycle to work scheme
One day a year paid voluntary / community work


At Sytner, our values and the way we behave are important to us. We are committed to creating an equitable working environment where we value and respect every individual's unique contribution, supporting our colleagues to thrive and achieve their full potential.

As part of our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion, you have the right to ask for changes / adjustments to job interviews and the recruitment process. For more information around reasonable adjustments and the recruitment process please click here.

Unsure? Read on…

We represent over 23 of the world's most prestige vehicle brands, across our 140 UK dealerships.

We pride ourselves on ‘Developing Talent and ‘Building Careers’ and our colleagues recently scored Sytner Group an outstanding 89% on our colleague engagement survey.

Title: Pharmacists - Fluent Speakers - AI Training - Nottingham, UK
Description:
Pharmacists - AI Training



About Prolific

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.

Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.

The role

We are looking for Licensed Pharmacists to help train and evaluate cutting-edge AI models. As AI begins to play a larger role in healthcare, these systems must be guided by pharmaceutical experts.

If you have the necessary experience, we'll send you a quick test to assess your skills and suitability for AI tasks. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a Domain Expert, where you'll be paid to train and evaluate powerful AI models.

Researchers looking for your skills pay up to $120 USD/hr per task completed, depending on skills and experience level. You must be prepared to complete tasks that require one hour of uninterrupted focus, though many are shorter.

What you'll bring


Verified Status: Current license/registration in good standing (e.g., PharmD, RPh, or equivalent) and board certification where applicable.
Clinical Expertise: Recent experience in retail, clinical, or hospital pharmacy, with comfort in evaluating drug interactions, dosing, and therapeutic reasoning.
Precision: Strong attention to detail and the ability to focus on complex medical data for up to one hour.
Technical Readiness: A reliable, fast internet connection, a computer, and a PayPal account to receive payments.
Independence: Willingness to self-declare earnings (participants are considered self-employed).
Assessment: Willingness to complete a short skills/eligibility screener to join our Domain Expert pool.


What you'll be doing in the role


Evaluating Accuracy: Reviewing AI-generated responses to pharmaceutical scenarios and rating them for accuracy, safety, and clinical reasoning.
Comparative Analysis: Comparing multiple model answers and selecting/justifying the best response based on current pharmacy standards.
Content Creation: Writing improved exemplars, rationales, or structured feedback to help models learn where they fall short in medication counseling or pharmacology.


Why Prolific is a great platform to join as a Participant

Joining our Expert Network will give you the chance to influence the AI models of the future using professional legal expertise. Once you pass our assessment, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems.

We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviours into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Links & Privacy

Website

Youtube

Privacy Statement

By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific's use of your personal information.

  • Evaluating Accuracy: Reviewing AI-generated responses to pharmaceutical scenarios and rating them for accuracy, safety, and clinical reasoning.
  • Comparative Analysis: Comparing multiple model answers and selecting/justifying the best response based on current pharmacy standards.
  • Content Creation: Writing improved exemplars, rationales, or structured feedback to help models learn where they fall short in medication counseling or pharmacology.

Why Prolific is a great platform to join as a Participant

Joining our Expert Network will give you the chance to influence the AI models of the future using professional legal expertise. Once you pass our assessment, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems.

We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviours into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Title: HTML/CSS - Senior Developers - AI Training - Nottingham, UK
Description:
HTML/CSS - Senior Developers - AI Training - UK

About Prolific

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.

Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.

The role

We're looking for Senior HTML/CSS Developers to join our Expert Network to help train and evaluate cutting-edge AI models. If you have a background in Software Engineering or Frontend Development, we'll send you a quick 10- to 15-minute test to assess your skills. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a participant, where you'll get paid to help AI understand and produce high-quality frontend code and design implementation.

Researchers looking for your skills tend to pay up to $40/hr, depending on skills and experience. You must be prepared to complete paid tasks that require one hour of uninterrupted work, though many are shorter.

What you'll bring


Educational Background: a BSc or higher in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Web Development, or a closely related technical field.
Professional Experience: real-world experience as a Senior Frontend Developer or UI Engineer specialising in HTML/CSS.
Coding Proficiency: ability to independently build complex, responsive layouts and components without relying on frameworks or libraries.
Deep Domain Knowledge: expert understanding of the CSS cascade, specificity, the box model, Flexbox, CSS Grid, accessibility standards (WCAG), and cross-browser compatibility.
Code Quality: a high standard for clean, semantic markup and maintainable CSS, including adherence to BEM methodology, design tokens, and modern CSS standards.


What you'll be doing in the role


Evaluate Code Accuracy: review AI-generated HTML/CSS for functional correctness, semantic structure, and adherence to best practices.
Validate Logic & Reasoning: audit the step-by-step explanations provided by AI for layout solutions and styling approaches to ensure they are technically sound.
Conduct Execution Testing: render model-generated markup and stylesheets in appropriate environments to verify visual output and cross-browser behaviour.
Annotate Model Performance: identify areas where a model produces inaccessible markup, inefficient selectors, deprecated attributes, or hallucinated CSS properties.
Refine Technical Logic: provide structured feedback on how models reason through responsive design, component architecture, or design system implementation.


Key Technologies


Core Languages: expert mastery of HTML5 and CSS3/4, including custom properties, animations, and modern layout systems.
Frameworks & Methodologies: extensive experience with Tailwind CSS, SASS/SCSS, or CSS Modules, and familiarity with component-driven design systems.
Testing & Tools: proficiency with browser DevTools, accessibility auditing tools (e.g., axe, Lighthouse), and cross-browser testing platforms.


Why Prolific is a great platform to join as a Participant

Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to influence the AI models of the future using professional legal expertise. Once you pass our assessment, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems.

We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviours into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Business Intelligence Analyst

Function: Commercial - Strategy, Performance, and Business Management

Location: Hybrid - one of our offices (Nottingham, Wakefield, Alfreton, Daresbury or Sheffield)

Contract type: Permanent

Salary: £37,502 with the potential to rise to £41,669 over 3 years

Closing Date: Monday 1 June 2026

We have a fantastic opportunity for a Business Intelligence Analyst to join our team. You will support the delivery of a programme of business intelligence development and enable standardisation and production of business and market intelligence. Utilise software and data repositories to create bespoke solutions for stakeholders in line with standard templates for the presentation of data and analysis.

Devise methods for identifying data patterns and trends in available information sources for the consumption of the commercial teams.

Every day you will …


Responsible for interpretation, analysis and presentation of data using a variety of tools including: Excel, Access, SQL, Alteryx, Power BI, Cognos, Python, Power Apps, Power Automate.
Work with commercial directorates teams to establish insights requirements and then source from valid data sets
Use data from wide range of sources to analyse key themes and identify possible impacts on the business.
Run advanced and predictive analyses and perform model assessments, validation, and enhancement activities, using predictive analytics software tools
Collate and analyse data using pre-set tools, methods and formats. Involves working independently.
Manage basic aspects of the data management system with guidance from senior colleagues. This includes being responsible for developing or operating basic elements of the data management systems to deliver outcomes.


What can we offer you?

We Want To Reward You For Your Passion, Enthusiasm, And Hard Work So We Offer Much More Than a Competitive Salary


Hybrid working opportunities, giving you the flexibility to work collaboratively in the office and remotely.
We recognise our employees' hard work and contributions with annual bonus schemes, long service, and colleague recognition awards. 
27 days holiday plus bank holidays, with the option to purchase an additional 5 days.
We are dedicated to your development, through in-house training, support, and access to external qualifications to maximise your potential.
A focus on your well-being offering 1 day of paid well-being leave and free access to the 24/7 Employee Assistance Programme
Generous pension scheme (with us contributing 12% when you contribute 6%)
Access to our Flexible Benefits Scheme, where you can choose from a variety of benefits such as Life Insurance, Critical Illness Cover, Income Protection, Health Cash Plan, Dental Insurance, and additional pension contributions that suit you.
2 days of paid volunteering leave allowing you to give back to your community.
Access to many discounts from the Blue Light Card to NHS Discounts.


NHS Supply Chain, who are we?

Our role is to support the NHS to save lives and improve health. We are a part of the NHS family, and our role is to source, deliver and supply healthcare products, services and food for NHS trusts and healthcare organisations across England and Wales.

We serve every NHS Trust and operate a national network of distribution centres, managing relationships with more than a thousand suppliers and delivering more than 8,000,000 orders each year to more than 17,000 locations. Doing all of this on behalf of the NHS gives NHS staff more time to focus on their main priority of providing excellent patient care.

What skills will help you thrive in this role?


Be able to provide technical guidance when required on analysing data trends for us in reports to help guide decision making.
Have an understanding and effectively using standard software packages while providing technical guidance when needed.
Have experience using data visualisation tools such as Power BI and Cognos to create clear, impactful dashboards and reports, while offering guidance to others when needed.
Have an understanding of statistical techniques and how to apply them to business problems, offering technical input when more advanced analysis is required.
Have a working knowledge of data governance principles and ensure data accuracy, consistency, and compliance with relevant policies.
Be able to work collaboratively with stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into effective BI solutions, ensuring alignment with business goals.
Be committed to continuous learning and staying up to date with emerging BI tools, technologies, and best practices.


Our Inclusive Commitment

At NHS Supply Chain, we are committed to building an inclusive environment where difference is not only valued, but celebrated, giving everyone the opportunity to thrive in their career. Developing our people is key to our success, so if this role sounds like the right next step in your career but your experience doesn’t match perfectly with the job advert, we encourage you to still apply.

Struggling to complete our application form, and require additional support? Reach out to our Talent Acquisition team at careers@supplychain.nhs.uk who will be happy to help you with alternative ways to apply.

We reserve the right to close any vacancy from further submissions when we have received sufficient applications from which to make a shortlist. Please apply without delay if you wish to be considered for this role.

SCCL is a company Registered in England and Wales, with company number 10881715, to act as the management function of the NHS Supply Chain.

Title: Analytics Manager
Description:
Location: Nottingham, hybrid (this role could also be based in London)

Salary: From £53k+ & 10% discretionary bonus

Level: Manager




About the Role

We are looking for an Advanced Analytics Manager to join our Boots Media Group team. In this role, you will help evolve BMG’s insight and measurement proposition, using Boots’ first-party data to unlock smarter, more actionable analytics for Boots and its brand partners. You’ll play a key role in shaping the future of BMG IQ, driving innovation across measurement, AI, automation and customer-level insight to create meaningful commercial impact.




If you love variety, thrive in a fast-paced environment, and embrace change with energy, this could be the right role for you.




Why SMG?

At SMG, we hire for the future, which is fast-moving and changing shape. Do you have the potential to help shape our business? We’re looking for brilliant, diverse talent who want to grow with us—people who are curious, ambitious, and eager to learn, whether as specialists or across teams.




Our culture is everything. If you’re someone who stays honest, stays hungry, stays foolish, and has fun, you’ll feel right at home here.




We value those who take ownership of their growth and bring fresh perspectives. That’s why we’re committed to equity, inclusion, and building a place where everyone feels empowered to grow.




At SMG, it’s not just about filling a role but building the future together.

10% discretionary bonus
£1,800 yearly wellbeing fund (on top of your salary!)
Free Headspace subscription
£500 yearly “Learning Fund” for learning
4 extra Wellbeing Days off per year
4pm finishes every Friday
Flexible and hybrid working

Explore all our benefits here




Are We What You’re Looking For?




We move fast, adapt quickly, and like to challenge each other. We're looking for people who enjoy the buzz of change, the satisfaction of building something better, and the joy of working with a close-knit, values-driven team.

This isn’t for everyone, but if you thrive in this environment, we’d love to meet you!




A Little Bit About You

A deep understanding of how customer-level data, including behavioural and transactional data, can be used to generate insight and optimise media performance.




A strong conceptual grasp of statistical models, advanced analytics and modern AI applications, with the confidence to guide and review technical work without needing to be the primary coder.




Experience leading technical teams through complex projects, ensuring strong accountability, clear documentation and high-quality delivery.




Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate complex data and analytical outputs into clear, compelling narratives for non-technical audiences.




Familiarity with digital measurement tools and platforms, such as Google Analytics or Criteo, would be a plus.




An understanding of data pipelines and data warehouse environments would also be beneficial, helping you collaborate effectively with data engineering and platform teams.




Don’t meet every single requirement? We still want to hear from you. If you believe you’d thrive in this role, your unique perspective might be just what we’re looking for.




What You’ll Be Doing

Your days will be dynamic. You might find yourself:

Building BMG’s data roadmap, exploring the full potential of Boots’ customer-level data and moving beyond reactive requests to pioneer new retail media solutions.




Sharing and aligning plans with senior stakeholders across Boots and BMG, clearly showing how advanced analytics can unlock commercial growth and competitive advantage.




Driving the adoption of AI and automation within the team’s workflow, improving both methodological depth and operational speed.




Challenging data science methodologies and assumptions, encouraging the team to think beyond the “how” and focus on the “so what” behind their models.




Developing deeper metrics and measurement solutions for existing and emerging channels, helping clients get more strategic value from their campaigns.




Working with Boots Leadership, Boots CMA, the BMG Client and Innovation teams, and SMG Data Ops to scope and deliver new uses of data.




Our Values:

Stay honest. Stay hungry. Stay foolish. Have fun.

Explore what that means to us here




Useful Links Before You Apply:

Learn about our departments
Our hiring process
Life at SMG
FAQs




Still Curious?

We always send a confirmation email - check your spam if you don’t see it. Problems? Email: careers@smg.team

Our full data retention policy can be found here.

a wide variety of projects managing the Mechanical aspects of projects including HVAC (Heating, Ventilation & Air Conditioning), Plumbing and Pipework. More specifically:

Title: Head of AI Engineering
Description:
Role Purpose

Location- Ruddington, Nottinghamshire

Level - Strategic Leader

Department - Product R&D

Working Pattern - Remote unless based locally

Benefits - Benefits At Ideagen

Salary - To be discussed at next stage

Mazlan+ Models is Ideagen’s most significant investment in AI to date, building trusted, domain‑specific AI capabilities for regulated industries. As Head of AI Engineering, you will build Ideagen’s AI engineering function from the ground up and lead it end to end. This is a senior leadership role where you will shape the strategy, architecture, and culture behind a platform that will define the future of Ideagen’s products. You will lead multiple specialist teams, work closely with executive leadership, and be accountable for delivering AI systems that customers can rely on where accuracy and compliance are critical.

Responsibilities


Owning the end‑to‑end strategy, roadmap, and delivery for Ideagen’s AI engineering function across models, data, MLOps, agentic experiences, and domain expertise
Building and leading specialist teams across five AI practice areas and growing the organisation toward 30 to 40 engineers at scale
Setting the technical and architectural direction for the AI platform, including inference systems, orchestration, model adapters, and serving infrastructure
Establishing engineering standards and responsible AI practices that meet ISO 42001 and the expectations of regulated customers
Embedding AI‑native ways of working by setting the standard for modern, agent‑assisted engineering productivity across all teams
Owning senior vendor and cloud relationships and making strategic decisions on model and infrastructure partnerships
Representing Ideagen’s AI capabilities internally and externally, translating technical outcomes into clear business value
Taking accountability for programme budgets, investment decisions, and measurable return on AI engineering spend


Skills And Experience


You have led a substantial AI or ML engineering organisation and scaled teams across multiple specialist disciplines
You bring deep applied AI and LLM expertise and can make informed architectural trade‑offs with senior engineers
You have delivered production AI systems at scale, including MLOps, inference infrastructure, experimentation, and observability
You actively use modern AI‑assisted development tools yourself and can demonstrate AI‑native productivity in practice
You are comfortable operating at executive level and framing engineering decisions in commercial and strategic terms
You have experience working in regulated or safety‑critical environments where failure carries real consequences
You build strong leadership teams and invest in clear progression, development, and engineering culture
Experience with formal AI governance frameworks or domain knowledge in EHS, quality, or compliance is an advantage


About Ideagen

Ideagen is the invisible force behind many things we rely on every day - from keeping airplanes soaring in the sky, to ensuring the food on our tables is safe, to helping doctors and nurses care for the sick. So, when you think of Ideagen, think of it as the silent teammate that's always working behind the scenes to help those people who make our lives safer and better. Everyday millions of people are kept safe using Ideagen software. We have offices all over the world including America, Australia, Malaysia and India with people doing lots of different and exciting jobs.

We’re building a future-ready team, and AI is part of how we work smarter. If you're curious, adaptable and open to using AI to improve how you work, you’ll thrive at Ideagen!

Title: Psychology - Graduates - AI Training - Nottingham, UK
Description:
Psychology - Graduates - AI Training - UK

About Prolific

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.

Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.

The role

We're looking for people with Psychology experience to work as self-employed AI Trainers to help train and evaluate cutting-edge AI models from some of the world's biggest AI labs. If you have the necessary experience, we'll send you a quick 10- to 15-minute test to assess your skills and suitability for AI tasks. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a Domain Expert participant, where you'll get paid to train and evaluate powerful AI models.

Researchers looking for your skills tend to pay up to £50/hr, subject to the length of tasks available. If successful, you must be prepared to complete paid tasks that require one hour of uninterrupted work, though many are shorter.

What you'll bring


Background in Psychology and ideally a degree in Psychology or a closely related field
A solid understanding of psychological theory, research methods, and human behaviour
A willingness to take our skills verification test to assess your suitability for our Domain Expert participant pool
Strong attention to detail and the ability to concentrate on complex tasks for up to one hour at a time
A reliable and fast internet connection and access to a computer
A PayPal account to receive payment from our clients


What you'll be doing in the role


Completing AI training tasks such as analyzing, editing, and writing psychology-related content
Judging the performance of AI on psychology, behavioural science, and human cognition prompts
Improving cutting-edge AI models using your understanding of psychological theory and human behaviour


Why Prolific is a great platform to join as a Participant

Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to influence the AI models of the future using professional legal expertise. Once you pass our assessment, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems.

We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviours into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Links to more information on Prolific

Website

Youtube

Privacy Statement

By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific use of your personal personal information.

Title: Audi Expert
Description:
About The Role

A fantastic opportunity has arisen for an Audi Expert to join our team at Audi Nottingham.

This is an exciting and varied position, ideal for someone who is passionate about delivering a class-leading customer experience and building genuine, long-lasting relationships with our guests. As an Audi Expert, you will be the first point of contact for customers visiting our showroom.

You will initiate conversations, build rapport, and guide customers through their ownership journey — from initial enquiry through to vehicle handover and beyond. Your role is not simply to sell; it is to inspire confidence in the Audi brand and ensure every customer interaction is memorable for the right reasons.

Key responsibilities include promoting the full range of Audi vehicles, accessories, and ownership benefits, clearly articulating the brand's unique selling points; proactively presenting and selling Vehicle Connection and Service Plan products, ensuring customers understand the value and benefits of each; and managing a personal portfolio of customers, conducting regular follow-up contact to maintain engagement throughout the ownership lifecycle.

This is a full-time position which will typically include weekend working, in line with our commitment to providing customers with the highest possible levels of service.

About You

We are looking for a motivated and customer-focused individual who thrives in a dynamic retail environment. You do not need to come from an automotive background — what matters most is your attitude, your passion for people, and your commitment to excellence

You Will Ideally Be Able To Demonstrate

Previous experience in a customer-facing retail or service environment.


A genuine passion for delivering outstanding customer experiences, consistently and without compromise.
Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to engage confidently with a diverse range of customers.
A proactive, self-motivated approach with the drive to meet and exceed personal and team targets.
Enthusiasm for the Audi brand and a desire to develop deep product knowledge.
Excellent organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple customer relationships simultaneously.
Collaborative team player attributes, who supports colleagues and contributes positively to team morale.


This role is a fantastic opportunity for those who enjoy meeting people and working for a prestige brand in a highly successful and motivated team.

Why Sytner?

Sytner Group are delighted to provide an industry-leading benefits package.

We are passionate about continuous improvement and building an environment where everyone feels valued, appreciated and able to reach their full potential.


Enhanced Holiday Entitlement – 33 days inc. bank holidays
Industry-leading Maternity, Paternity and Adoption Pay
Career Development
Recognition of Long Service every 5 years
Discounted Car Schemes
High Street Discounts
Discounted Gym memberships
Cycle to work scheme
One day a year paid voluntary / community work


At Sytner, our values and the way we behave are important to us. We are committed to creating an equitable working environment where we value and respect every individual's unique contribution, supporting our colleagues to thrive and achieve their full potential.

As part of our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion, you have the right to ask for changes / adjustments to job interviews and the recruitment process. For more information around reasonable adjustments and the recruitment process please click here.

Unsure? Read on…

We represent over 23 of the world's most prestige vehicle brands, across our 140 UK dealerships.

We pride ourselves on ‘Developing Talent and ‘Building Careers’ and our colleagues recently scored Sytner Group an outstanding 89% on our colleague engagement survey

Title: Bioscience Operational Support Scientist (6-Month Fixed-Term Contract)
Description:
About Sygnature Discovery

At Sygnature Discovery, we create exceptional scientific outcomes. For our customers, who bring life-changing medicines to patients; and for our people, who help make this possible.

We’re 700+ scientists from over 50 countries, working as one across Europe and North America. With no competing internal programs, we offer total confidentiality and commitment.

Over the past 20 years, we’ve earned a track record of real impact: 60+ advanced candidates, 200+ patents filed, 200+ active projects. Over 90% of our customers choose to stay with us – from biotechs racing to clinic to pharma companies that need specialised expertise for targeted challenges.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly organised and practical Bioscience Operational Support Scientist to support the smooth operation of our scientific laboratories. You will play a pivotal role in supporting our Bioscientists, ensuring they have the resources they need to carry out scientific research. This role is essential in ensuring equipment readiness, reagent availability, and tissue culture support across multiple Bioscience capabilities.

You will work closely with scientists, our facilities team, and specialist "superusers" to maintain laboratory infrastructure, manage consumables, support audits, and assist with cell culture and project activities.

Please note that this is a full-time onsite position based at our BioCity, Nottingham offices and is a 6-month fixed-term contract.

Role Responsibilities


Support instrument superusers with routine maintenance, data archiving, specialist stock inventories and hosting service engineers.
Support our Reagent Management Team with reagent stock checks, ordering and storage management.
Tissue culture support including cell line banking, cell shipments, Mycoplasma testing and STR profiling.
Support for cell line audits, cryostore management and laboratory compliance tasks.
Coordination of Pipette maintenance, repairs and servicing.
Support for project closure procedures, including reagent shipments and disposal.
Laboratory, project and compound management support as required.
Compliance to Health and Safety regulations.


Skills & Qualifications:


Degree or equivalent experience in a Bioscience-related discipline.
Familiarity with laboratory environments, equipment and standard laboratory practices.
Experience working with tissue culture is highly desirable.
Strong organisational skills and attention to detail.
Ability to follow SOPs and work safely in a regulated environment.
Good communication skills and ability to work collaboratively with multidisciplinary teams.
Willingness to manage varied tasks with changing priorities.


Benefits (may be reduced pro-rata)


🌴 25 days annual leave (plus bank holidays)
🏥 Private Medical Insurance
🛡️ Life Insurance
🎯 Employee Assistance Programme
⚖️ Enhanced Family Friendly Policies
💰 Sygnature Group Pension Scheme
💚 1 paid volunteer day per year


Please note: This advert may close early if we receive a high volume of applications. We encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible to avoid missing out on this opportunity.

Title: United Kingdom Residents - Survey Participants - Nottingham, UK
Description:
Survey Participants - UK

About Prolific

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.

Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.

The role

We are looking for Study Participants from all professional backgrounds across the UK to take part in academic and applied research studies for global businesses and educational faculties.

Whether you are just starting your career or are a seasoned expert, your perspective is valuable. We are actively seeking a diverse range of educational backgrounds, including:


No High School Diploma / High School Graduates
Trade School & Vocational Professionals (MBO)
Undergraduate & Master's Degree Holders (HBO/WO)
PhD Candidates & Doctoral Researchers


If you have the necessary experience, we'll send you a quick 10- to 15-minute screening to assess your suitability. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a participant, where you'll get paid to contribute to a wide range of projects.

Compensation: Researchers looking for your profile tend to pay up to $18/hr per study completed. While many tasks are short, you should be prepared for studies that require up to one hour of uninterrupted work.

What you'll be doing in the role

As a participant, you will contribute to high-quality research outcomes by responding thoughtfully to various tasks, including:


AI Training & Evaluation: helping refine Large Language Models by ranking responses or providing creative prompts.
Behavioral Experiments: participating in decision-making tasks and cognitive games.
User Research: providing feedback on new software, apps, or product concepts.
Academic Studies: assisting university researchers in fields like psychology, economics, and sociology.
Interviews: engaging in one-on-one deep dives regarding your professional expertise or lived experience.
Surveys & Questionnaires: sharing your opinions on current events, consumer habits, or workplace culture.


What you'll bring


Diverse Perspective: a track record of relevant experience, knowledge, or lived context (from entry-level roles to executive or academic positions).
Attention to Detail: the ability to remain focused and provide high-quality data for the duration of a study.
Technical Setup: a reliable, fast internet connection and access to a desktop or laptop computer.
Financial Readiness: a PayPal account to receive payments and a willingness to self-declare earnings as a self-employed participant.


Why Join Prolific?

Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to contribute to meaningful research used by universities, researchers, and global organizations. Once you pass our screening, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and organizations with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data helps researchers answer important questions, improve products and services, and advance knowledge across disciplines.

We believe that the best research outcomes come from diverse human perspectives and experiences. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of research innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific's use of your personal information.
